generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via role-based access control (RBAC) aligned to HSEEP-defined exercise roles, including Exercise Director, Controller, Evaluator, and Player, enforcing least-privilege access to all display features and data.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP doctrine and NIST SP 800-53 AC control family require role-differentiated access to prevent unauthorized viewing or manipulation of sensitive exercise data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict data modification actions exclusively to authorized roles (e.g., Exercise Director, Controller), preventing Evaluator and Player roles from altering any exercise data through the display tool or its deep-links.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege enforcement ensures that read-only roles cannot inadvertently or maliciously alter live exercise state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically refresh all displayed data — including the timeline view and data table — at a configurable polling interval bounded between 10 seconds and 5 minutes, with a default interval of 30 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable, bounded refresh ensures data currency while allowing administrators to tune load against network and server capacity during full-scale exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a persistent 'Last Updated' timestamp and a visual staleness indicator on all screens, changing the indicator to a warning state if data has not been successfully refreshed within two consecutive polling intervals.
generated_requirements.rationale: Coordinators must be immediately aware of stale data to avoid making incorrect inject decisions during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each data refresh cycle — from initiating the upstream API request to rendering updated content in both the timeline and data table — within 5 seconds under normal operating conditions with up to 500 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict latency tolerance is required in live exercise environments where delayed data can cause coordinators to issue incorrect or untimely injects.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the Sync Matrix application via a documented, versioned API or data exchange standard, and shall detect and alert administrators upon any breaking change or version mismatch in the data contract.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned API contracts prevent silent data contract breakage that could corrupt the display without operator awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the Exercise Management Tool via a documented, versioned API or data exchange standard, and shall detect and alert administrators upon any breaking change or version mismatch in the data contract.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent interoperability with both upstream sources is essential to maintain a single, authoritative integrated view for exercise coordinators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully degrade when one or both upstream data sources (Sync Matrix, Exercise Management Tool) are unavailable, displaying the most recently cached data, a prominent connectivity-loss banner, and the timestamp of the last successful refresh, without crashing or presenting blank screens.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation ensures coordinators retain situational awareness from cached data and are not left without any display during upstream outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a dual-region interface consisting of a graphical timeline view and a structured data table view, both visible simultaneously on a single screen without requiring scrolling to access either region on displays of 1920×1080 resolution or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: The dual-region layout is the core UI paradigm enabling coordinators to correlate temporal and tabular exercise data at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The timeline view shall map directly to Master Scenario Events List (MSEL) inject identifiers, displaying each inject's expected time, actual time, responsible controller, and venue on the graphical timeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Direct MSEL alignment ensures the timeline is authoritative and traceable to the exercise design artifacts required by HSEEP doctrine.

generated_requirements.text: The data table view shall display, at minimum, the following columns for each Event or Activity: MSEL inject ID, event/activity name, scheduled start time, actual start time, responsible controller, assigned venue/cell, current status, and schedule-alignment color indicator.
generated_requirements.rationale: A standardized column set ensures all operationally critical fields are consistently visible to exercise management personnel.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply color-coded schedule-alignment indicators to each Event and Activity according to the following documented criteria: Green — activity is on schedule (actual time within ±2 minutes of scheduled time); Yellow — activity is moderately delayed or early (between 2 and 10 minutes variance); Red — activity is significantly off schedule (greater than 10 minutes variance); Grey — activity has not yet started.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit, documented thresholds for color-coding ensure consistent interpretation of schedule alignment across all users and exercises.

generated_requirements.text: All color-coded status indicators shall conform to WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements and shall supplement color with a secondary visual indicator (e.g., icon, pattern, or text label) so that users with color vision deficiencies can distinguish all status states without relying on color alone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance is mandatory for federal agency deployments and ensures accessibility in high-stress operational environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide multi-select venue and cell filtering in both the timeline and data table views, allowing users to simultaneously filter by one or more venues or cells to focus on relevant exercise segments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-select filtering is essential for large-scale, multi-venue exercises where coordinators need to isolate specific operational areas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to save, name, and reload filter profiles (venue, cell, event type, status) on a per-user-role basis, with each user able to store a minimum of 10 named filter profiles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Saved filter profiles reduce setup time and cognitive load for recurring roles across multi-day exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to customize the data table view by reordering, showing, or hiding columns, and shall persist these customizations per user account across sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistent column customization supports diverse role-specific information needs without requiring system reconfiguration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide deep-link navigation from any Event or Activity record in the display tool directly to the corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix or Exercise Management Tool), opening the source application in a new browser tab or window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deep-links enable coordinators to navigate directly to the authoritative source for data modifications without manual searching.

generated_requirements.text: Deep-link navigation to source applications shall include an authenticated session handoff using a federated identity mechanism (e.g., SAML 2.0 or OAuth 2.0), so that users are not required to re-authenticate when transitioning to the source application, provided their session is still valid.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless authentication handoff reduces friction during time-critical exercise execution while maintaining security posture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display exercise security classification markings (e.g., FOUO, CUI, or exercise-specific handling caveats) persistently in the header and footer of every screen, and shall apply the highest applicable marking present in the currently displayed dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persistent classification markings are required under 32 CFR Part 2002 CUI handling rules and HSEEP security guidance for exercises involving sensitive scenario details.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and retain a complete audit trail of all user interactions, including login/logout events, filter changes, column customizations, view switches, deep-link activations, and export actions, recording the user identity, timestamp, and action details for each event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit trails satisfy NIST SP 800-53 AU control family requirements and support post-exercise After Action Review accountability.

generated_requirements.text: Audit trail records shall be retained for the duration of the exercise plus a minimum of three years post-exercise, in conformance with NARA General Records Schedule (GRS) requirements for emergency preparedness records, and shall be stored in a tamper-evident format.
generated_requirements.rationale: NARA GRS compliance and tamper-evidence are required for federal records management and post-exercise legal or programmatic review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture historical snapshots of the complete exercise display state (all Events, Activities, statuses, and filter configurations) at a configurable interval (default: every 15 minutes) throughout the exercise, and shall store these snapshots for post-exercise AAR reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Periodic state snapshots enable reconstruction of exercise execution timelines for HSEEP-mandated After Action Review and Improvement Plan deliverables.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a historical reporting module that allows authorized users to query, view, and export exercise state snapshots for any time range within a completed or ongoing exercise, with export formats including PDF and CSV.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable historical reports in standard formats support AAR/IP deliverable preparation and stakeholder distribution as required by HSEEP doctrine.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent monitoring of at least 500 simultaneous Events and Activities across a minimum of 50 venues without degradation in display refresh performance or UI responsiveness.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full-scale homeland security exercises may involve hundreds of simultaneous injects across multiple venues over multi-day periods, requiring high-capacity display support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of 99.9% or greater during scheduled exercise windows, as measured over any rolling 30-day period, with planned maintenance windows scheduled exclusively outside of exercise operational periods.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during exercise windows is critical because system downtime directly impairs exercise execution and coordinator decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any unplanned outage and restore full display functionality, including live data refresh, within 5 minutes (Recovery Time Objective), with a Recovery Point Objective of no more than one polling interval of data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tight RTO and RPO bounds ensure that exercise execution is minimally disrupted by system failures during live operations.

generated_requirements.text: All user interface components — including the timeline, data table, filters, modals, and export dialogs — shall comply with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act and WCAG 2.1 AA standards, including full keyboard navigability and screen reader compatibility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 508 compliance is a legal requirement for federal agency deployments and ensures equitable access for users with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce transport-layer encryption (TLS 1.2 or higher) for all data in transit between the display tool, upstream source applications, and end-user browsers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit is required by NIST SP 800-53 SC controls to protect CUI and sensitive exercise data from interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data at rest — including cached exercise data, audit logs, historical snapshots, and user profile data — using AES-256 or an equivalent FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic mechanism.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects CUI and sensitive exercise scenario data in compliance with NIST SP 800-53 SC and 32 CFR Part 2002 requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity of all data received from upstream sources (Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool) by checking for schema conformance, required field completeness, and referential integrity against known MSEL inject identifiers before rendering data on screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity validation prevents corrupted or malformed upstream data from being displayed as authoritative exercise state, satisfying NIST SP 800-53 SI controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a non-dismissible data integrity warning banner when received data fails validation checks, identifying the affected data source and the nature of the validation failure, without hiding or replacing the last known valid data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visible integrity warnings ensure coordinators are not misled by invalid data while retaining access to the last known good state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Exercise Directors to configure the color-coding threshold values (e.g., the minute boundaries for Green/Yellow/Red status) on a per-exercise basis through an administrative settings interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable thresholds accommodate varying exercise tempo and inject timing tolerances across different exercise types and scales.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a keyword search capability within the data table view, allowing users to search across all visible columns and highlight matching rows in real time as the user types.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time keyword search enables rapid location of specific injects or activities during fast-paced exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The timeline view shall support horizontal scrolling and zoom controls (minimum zoom range: 15-minute to 24-hour time window) to allow users to navigate across multi-day exercise schedules and focus on specific time segments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Zoom and scroll controls are necessary to navigate multi-day exercise timelines without losing context of the overall schedule.

generated_requirements.text: Selecting an Event or Activity in the timeline view shall synchronously highlight and scroll the corresponding row in the data table view, and vice versa, maintaining bidirectional selection synchronization between the two regions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bidirectional synchronization between views reduces cognitive load and navigation time when correlating timeline and tabular data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent authenticated users without exceeding the 5-second end-to-end refresh latency requirement, as validated by load testing prior to each major exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large-scale exercises involve many simultaneous users across venues, and performance must be validated under realistic load conditions before go-live.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all API calls made to upstream source applications, including request timestamps, response codes, response latencies, and any error payloads, and shall make these logs available to system administrators for diagnostics.
generated_requirements.rationale: API call logging supports rapid diagnosis of integration failures and satisfies NIST SP 800-53 AU audit requirements for system-to-system interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative dashboard accessible only to system administrators and Exercise Directors, displaying real-time system health metrics including upstream API connectivity status, current polling interval, active user count, and cache freshness.
generated_requirements.rationale: An administrative health dashboard enables rapid identification and response to system or integration issues during live exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard federal government infrastructure (on-premises or FedRAMP-authorized cloud), and shall not require proprietary hardware or operating system dependencies beyond a modern web browser on the client side.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability to standard federal infrastructure and browser-based client access ensures broad deployability across diverse agency environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the full HSEEP exercise design lifecycle phases — Design and Development, Conduct, and Evaluation — by providing phase-appropriate views and data filters that surface relevant MSEL, inject, and evaluation data for each phase.
generated_requirements.rationale: Alignment with HSEEP lifecycle phases ensures the tool supports exercise management from planning through AAR without requiring separate systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to annotate individual Events or Activities with free-text controller notes during exercise execution, with annotations timestamped, attributed to the annotating user, and included in historical snapshots and AAR exports.
generated_requirements.rationale: Controller annotations capture real-time observations that are critical inputs to the After Action Review and Improvement Plan process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement session timeout after a configurable period of user inactivity (default: 15 minutes), requiring re-authentication before resuming access, in compliance with NIST SP 800-53 AC-11.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access to CUI exercise data from unattended workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a printable and PDF-exportable summary report of the current display state (active filters, timeline snapshot, and data table contents) that includes all persistent classification markings and a generation timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Printable reports support situational briefings and documentation needs during and after exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase, APIs, and data schemas shall be documented sufficiently to allow a qualified developer to onboard, extend, or modify the system within 30 days, with all documentation maintained in a version-controlled repository alongside the source code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Well-maintained documentation reduces long-term maintenance costs and supports continuity across personnel changes in federal program offices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify system administrators via email or integrated alerting within 60 seconds when an upstream data source has been unreachable for more than two consecutive polling intervals.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting enables administrators to investigate and resolve upstream connectivity issues before they materially impact exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts, consistent with NIST SP 800-53 IA-2 requirements for federal information systems handling CUI.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA is required by NIST SP 800-53 IA controls to protect access to systems processing CUI and sensitive exercise scenario data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the data table view to be sorted by any column in ascending or descending order, with the current sort state visually indicated in the column header and preserved within the user's session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible sorting enables coordinators to prioritize viewing of the most time-critical or status-critical events during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a current exercise clock — synchronized to an authoritative time source (e.g., NTP) — prominently in the interface header, showing both exercise time and wall-clock time where they differ (e.g., accelerated or compressed exercise timelines).
generated_requirements.rationale: An authoritative, synchronized exercise clock is essential for accurate schedule-alignment calculations and coordinator situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo automated regression testing covering all integration points, RBAC rules, color-coding logic, and data refresh workflows prior to each exercise deployment, with test results documented and retained as part of the system's configuration management records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated regression testing ensures that updates or configuration changes do not silently break critical exercise management functionality before live deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a user-accessible help system — including contextual tooltips, a searchable user guide, and role-specific quick-reference cards — accessible without leaving the application, covering all major features including filtering, timeline navigation, deep-links, and export functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated help resources reduce training burden and support rapid onboarding of exercise personnel who may use the system infrequently across exercise cycles.

sme_advisory: This system functions as a real-time exercise command-and-control display layer within the HSEEP framework, integrating with authoritative data sources (Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool) to support exercise execution monitoring. Requirements engineers must pay close attention to HSEEP compliance obligations, role-based access control tied to exercise security classifications, and the strict latency tolerances expected in live exercise environments where delayed or stale data can cause coordinators to make incorrect inject decisions. Special attention should be given to interoperability contracts with upstream data sources, graceful degradation when those feeds are unavailable, and the auditability of all display states for post-exercise After Action Review (AAR) purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate realtors using multi-factor authentication (MFA), verifying both credentials and active MLS membership status before granting access to any application features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access must be restricted to licensed, active MLS participants as mandated by MLS participant agreements and NAR Code of Ethics obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that agent-only data fields — including agent remarks, showing instructions, and commission splits — are visible exclusively to verified, active MLS member realtors and are never exposed to unlicensed parties.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS participant agreements and NAR Code of Ethics prohibit sharing confidential listing fields with unlicensed individuals, making field-level RBAC a compliance requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to search MLS listings using filters including property type, price range, number of bedrooms and bathrooms, square footage, lot size, geographic area, and listing status, returning results that conform to RESO Data Dictionary field names and data types.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS search is the core field workflow for realtors, and RESO compliance ensures interoperability with MLS systems and third-party integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display property listing detail pages that include all MLS-required fields, high-resolution property images, an interactive map showing the property location, required MLS/board logos, agent attribution, and a 'last updated' timestamp as mandated by the applicable MLS participant agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS participant agreements mandate specific attribution and branding elements on all listing displays to maintain data licensing compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a mapping and directions service to provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current GPS location to any selected property address.
generated_requirements.rationale: Field-based realtors require in-app navigation to efficiently travel between properties without switching to external applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable realtors to generate Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) reports by selecting comparable sold properties filterable by sold date range, proximity radius, property type, square footage tolerance (±percentage), and lot size; each generated report must include a prominent disclaimer stating that the valuation is not a licensed appraisal.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a primary value-delivery workflow for realtors, and the appraisal disclaimer is required to avoid misrepresentation under state real estate licensing laws.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to initiate a phone call to any client contact directly from within the application using the device's native calling capability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Direct in-app calling supports field-based communication workflows without requiring realtors to leave the application context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to compose and send appointment requests to clients, specifying property address, proposed date and time, and optional notes, and shall integrate with showing management services (e.g., ShowingTime, Aligned Showings) to handle confirmation, cancellation, and post-showing feedback workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment and showing request management is a standard field workflow expectation for realtors and requires integration with established showing management platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall receive, display, and process appointment responses (accept, decline, reschedule) from clients and counterparties, updating the realtor's calendar in real time and triggering push notifications upon receipt of each response.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely processing of appointment responses is critical to field scheduling efficiency and prevents missed or double-booked showings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform bi-directional synchronization of contact records and calendar events with the realtor's office back-end CRM and calendar systems, handling deduplication, field mapping conflicts, and supporting a configurable sync frequency of no less than every 15 minutes when the device is connected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bi-directional sync ensures realtors always have current contact and scheduling data regardless of where updates originate, reducing data inconsistency risks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a fully functional offline mode in which realtors can access previously cached MLS listings, client contacts, calendar events, and in-progress CMA reports without a network connection, with cached listing data not exceeding the maximum staleness threshold permitted by the applicable MLS participant agreement (default: 24 hours).
generated_requirements.rationale: Field realtors frequently operate in areas with poor connectivity, and offline access to core data is essential for continuous productivity while remaining MLS-compliant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement an offline-to-online conflict resolution mechanism that, upon reconnection, detects write conflicts between locally modified records and server-side records, presents the realtor with a conflict resolution prompt for contact and calendar data, and applies a last-write-wins policy for non-critical cached listing data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Without explicit conflict resolution, offline edits risk silently overwriting or being overwritten by server-side changes, leading to data integrity failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize MLS listing data using incremental (delta) feed updates via the RESO Web API rather than full data pulls, fetching only records modified since the last successful sync timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Incremental sync reduces bandwidth consumption and latency on mobile networks, improving performance and reducing data costs for field users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver push notifications to realtors for time-sensitive events including new listings matching saved searches, appointment confirmations and cancellations, and incoming client messages, with automatic fallback to SMS or email if push delivery fails within 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reliable notification delivery for time-sensitive real estate events is critical, and fallback mechanisms ensure realtors are not left uninformed due to push delivery failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property searches with defined filter criteria and receive automated alerts when new MLS listings matching those criteria become available.
generated_requirements.rationale: Saved search alerts enable proactive client service by notifying realtors of relevant new inventory without requiring manual re-searches.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to create, view, edit, and delete client contact records, including name, phone numbers, email addresses, mailing address, client type (buyer/seller/both), and notes, subject to data minimization principles under applicable state privacy laws.
generated_requirements.rationale: Client relationship management is a core field workflow, and data minimization compliance is required under CCPA and similar state privacy regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide clients with mechanisms to exercise their privacy rights, including the ability to request access to their personal data held in the system and to request deletion of their personal data, with the realtor receiving and being able to action such requests within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: CCPA and other state privacy laws grant consumers rights of access and deletion over their personal data, requiring the system to support these workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include opt-out mechanisms in all automated appointment reminder and SMS communications sent through the platform, and shall honor and persist do-not-contact preferences for each client record in compliance with TCPA requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: TCPA mandates opt-out mechanisms and do-not-contact compliance for automated communications, with violations carrying significant per-message penalties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce IDX display rules when surfacing listing data in any consumer-facing context, including displaying all required fields, suppressing prohibited fields, and automatically expiring or refreshing displayed listings in accordance with the applicable MLS board's IDX policy refresh requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: IDX policy violations can result in termination of MLS data access, making automated enforcement of display rules a critical compliance requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be self-installing on supported mobile platforms, requiring the realtor to complete installation and initial account setup — including MLS credential entry and office system connection — through an in-app guided workflow of no more than five steps without requiring IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The self-installing, zero-IT-assistance requirement is an explicit design constraint that directly impacts onboarding adoption rates among field realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to export CMA reports as PDF documents suitable for client presentation, shareable via email or messaging directly from the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: PDF export enables realtors to deliver professional client-ready presentations from the field without requiring desktop access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an interactive map view of MLS search results, allowing realtors to pan, zoom, and tap individual listing pins to access property summary cards, in both connected and cached-offline modes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Map-based listing exploration is a primary spatial workflow for field realtors assessing neighborhood inventory and proximity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all access to agent-only MLS data fields, including the authenticated user identity, timestamp, and data fields accessed, and retain these audit logs for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging of sensitive field access supports MLS compliance audits and provides an evidentiary trail in the event of a data misuse investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically revoke application access and invalidate cached credentials for any realtor whose MLS membership status becomes inactive or suspended, within one hour of the status change being reflected in the MLS membership data feed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prompt access revocation upon MLS membership lapse is required to prevent unauthorized access to restricted listing data by non-active participants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to MLS listing search queries and return results within 3 seconds under normal network conditions (4G LTE or Wi-Fi) for result sets of up to 200 listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Field realtors require fast search response times to maintain productivity during client interactions and property tours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render property detail pages, including images and interactive maps, within 4 seconds on a 4G LTE connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Slow property page rendering degrades the client-facing presentation experience during field showings and consultations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and render a CMA report containing up to 20 comparable properties within 10 seconds of the realtor initiating the report generation action.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a time-sensitive workflow often performed in the presence of clients, requiring a responsive turnaround to maintain professional credibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent authenticated users without degradation in response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: The platform must support thousands of simultaneous field users across potentially large regional MLS markets without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all sensitive data at rest on the device — including cached listing data, client PII, and authentication tokens — using AES-256 encryption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mobile devices are at elevated risk of loss or theft, making strong encryption of both transit and at-rest data essential to protect MLS data and client PII.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock and require re-authentication after 10 minutes of user inactivity, and shall provide a remote wipe capability for device-resident application data that can be triggered by an administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-lock and remote wipe mitigate the risk of unauthorized data access in the event of device loss or theft in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for all third-party service integrations (MLS, CRM, calendar, mapping, showing management), ensuring that third-party credentials are never stored in plaintext on the device.
generated_requirements.rationale: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE is the current security best practice for mobile application third-party authorization, preventing credential exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify affected realtors and, where required by applicable state law, relevant authorities within 72 hours of discovery of a data breach involving client PII or MLS data stored or processed by the platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: CCPA and various state breach notification laws impose strict timelines for notifying affected individuals and regulators following a data breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be operable by a first-time user without any formal training, achieving task completion for core workflows (search, view listing, generate CMA, schedule appointment) within 5 minutes of first launch, as validated by usability testing with a representative sample of realtors.
generated_requirements.rationale: The zero-training onboarding requirement imposes a measurable usability constraint that must be validated through testing rather than assumed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual in-app guidance, tooltips, and an embedded help system accessible from every screen, enabling realtors to self-resolve common usage questions without contacting support.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-service help supports the zero-training design constraint and reduces support burden for a geographically distributed field user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, ensuring the application is usable by realtors with visual, motor, or cognitive impairments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance broadens the usable audience and may be required under applicable disability non-discrimination laws.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an application uptime of 99.5% or greater, measured on a rolling 30-day basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Field realtors depend on the application during client-facing activities where downtime directly impacts business outcomes and professional reputation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement graceful degradation such that, in the event of back-end service unavailability, the application continues to function in offline mode using cached data and queues outbound actions for retry upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation ensures continuous field productivity even during partial back-end outages, which is critical for a mobile-first application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully functional on iOS (current and one prior major version) and Android (current and one prior major version) mobile operating systems, with a consistent feature set and user experience across both platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: The realtor user base uses a mix of iOS and Android devices, and multi-platform support is an explicit system requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support installation and operation on devices with a minimum of 3 GB RAM and 64 GB storage, and shall not require device rooting or jailbreaking to install or operate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defining minimum device specifications ensures consistent performance across the diverse hardware landscape of the target user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with MLS data sources via the RESO Web API standard, ensuring that all property data field mappings conform to the RESO Data Dictionary, and shall support connection to multiple regional MLS boards simultaneously for realtors holding multi-board membership.
generated_requirements.rationale: RESO Web API compliance ensures interoperability with the broad ecosystem of MLS systems and reduces integration risk for multi-board realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system's back-end services shall be architected using loosely coupled, independently deployable microservices or modules, such that updates to individual components (e.g., MLS sync engine, CMA calculator, notification service) can be deployed without requiring full application redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular architecture reduces deployment risk and enables rapid response to MLS policy changes or third-party API updates without system-wide disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain comprehensive API documentation and integration guides for all external system touchpoints (MLS, CRM, calendar, showing management, mapping), updated within 30 days of any interface change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Up-to-date integration documentation reduces the time and risk associated with onboarding new MLS boards or updating third-party integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a mean time to recovery (MTTR) of no more than 2 hours for critical service failures affecting core workflows (authentication, MLS search, synchronization).
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined MTTR target ensures that service restoration is prioritized and measurable, limiting the business impact of critical failures on field realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity of all MLS data received via the RESO Web API feed, rejecting and logging records with missing required fields, invalid data types, or values outside defined RESO Data Dictionary constraints, without crashing or corrupting the local data cache.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS feed data quality is variable across boards, and robust validation prevents corrupt data from propagating into the application's cache and CMA calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data minimization by collecting and storing on the device only the client PII fields strictly necessary for the realtor's active workflows, and shall purge cached client PII from the device within 30 days of the client relationship being marked inactive.
generated_requirements.rationale: CCPA and state privacy laws require data minimization and retention limits for consumer PII, reducing both legal exposure and breach impact.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce MLS-board-specific offline caching policies, including maximum cache duration and restrictions on data export, configurable per MLS board, to ensure compliance with each board's participant agreement terms.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS participant agreements vary by region and may impose different caching duration limits; a configurable enforcement mechanism prevents inadvertent violations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative console enabling designated office administrators to manage realtor accounts, configure office system integration settings, review audit logs, and trigger remote data wipes, accessible via a web browser without requiring mobile device access.
generated_requirements.rationale: An administrative console enables office-level governance of the platform, supporting account lifecycle management and compliance oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support push notification delivery with an end-to-end delivery latency of no more than 30 seconds from event trigger to device notification display under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Time-sensitive real estate events such as showing confirmations and new matching listings require near-real-time notification to be actionable for field realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a clear, plain-language privacy notice to realtors during onboarding, disclosing what client PII is collected, how it is used, with whom it is shared, and how realtors can exercise data subject rights on behalf of their clients.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent privacy disclosure is required under CCPA and other state privacy laws and supports informed consent by both realtors and their clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support localization and regional configuration, allowing MLS-board-specific rules (display requirements, cache policies, IDX restrictions, required attribution) to be applied dynamically based on the MLS board(s) associated with the authenticated realtor's membership.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS rules vary significantly by region and board; dynamic per-board configuration prevents a single hardcoded ruleset from causing compliance failures across multi-board deployments.

sme_subject: licensed real estate broker and MLS integration specialist
sme_advisory: This mobile MLS-integrated realtor application sits at the intersection of tightly regulated real estate data ecosystems, field-based mobility requirements, and enterprise-grade security demands. Requirements engineers must pay close attention to MLS board licensing rules and RESO data standards, which govern how listing data can be displayed, cached, and shared — violations can result in MLS access termination. Equally critical are the offline-sync architecture, CMA calculation accuracy, and cross-platform portability concerns, all of which have well-known failure modes in field deployments that must be explicitly addressed in both functional and non-functional requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, edit, and archive cohorts of nursing students, capturing cohort name, start quarter, associated Program of Study, and current status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cohort lifecycle management is the foundational administrative workflow described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and version Programs of Study, specifying the ordered sequence of classroom and clinical courses, credit hours, prerequisite chains, and required clinical hours per specialty rotation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Programs of Study are the curriculum backbone that drives scheduling logic and accreditation documentation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce prerequisite chains defined in the Program of Study as hard constraints, preventing enrollment of a student in any course for which all prerequisites have not been recorded as successfully completed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Quarter-aware prerequisite enforcement is required by Illinois Nurse Practice Act progression rules and ACEN/CCNE curriculum standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduling of both classroom courses and clinical courses across multiple quarters, associating each scheduled section with a cohort, instructor, time block, location or clinical site, and quarter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-quarter scheduling across course types is the primary operational function of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect and flag scheduling conflicts — including instructor double-booking, student double-booking, lab section overlap, and clinical site capacity overrun — within the same time block, preventing the conflicting assignment from being saved without explicit override by a Program Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated conflict detection prevents scheduling errors that would disrupt student progression and violate site affiliation agreements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track each clinical site's affiliation agreement, storing the contract effective date, expiration date, maximum concurrent student slots, and approved specialty areas, and shall prevent scheduling of any student to a site whose affiliation agreement has expired or whose approved specialty does not match the assigned rotation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical affiliation agreements are legally binding contracts; scheduling students outside their terms creates institutional liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall alert Program Administrators via in-application notification and email no fewer than 90 days before a clinical site's affiliation agreement expiration date.
generated_requirements.rationale: Advance warning allows administrators to renew agreements before they lapse and disrupt clinical placements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track each student's cumulative clinical hours per specialty rotation and compare them against the program-defined minimums, generating an automated alert to the assigned Program Administrator and Nursing Staff Member when a student's projected hours fall below the required minimum for any rotation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Illinois Nurse Practice Act and ACEN/CCNE standards impose minimum clinical hour requirements that must be monitored continuously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail for all create, update, and delete operations on schedule records, cohort assignments, clinical site assignments, and student progress records, capturing the acting user's identity, timestamp, affected record identifier, field name, prior value, and new value.
generated_requirements.rationale: ACEN/CCNE accreditation site visits require documented traceability of all curriculum and placement changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated Audit Trail viewer, accessible only to Program Administrators and Accreditation Reviewers, that supports filtering by date range, user, record type, and affected entity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accreditation reviewers must be able to retrieve specific change histories efficiently during site visits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with at minimum four distinct roles — Program Administrator, Nursing Staff Member, Instructor, and Accreditation Reviewer — each with a precisely defined set of permitted read and write operations on every data entity.
generated_requirements.rationale: FERPA and institutional policy require least-privilege access to protect sensitive student and clinical site records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict Instructors to read-only access of their own assigned course sections, student rosters for those sections, and relevant schedule information, and shall not expose cohort-level administrative data, clinical site contract details, or other instructors' records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege enforcement for Instructors prevents unauthorized access to FERPA-protected and contractually sensitive data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict Accreditation Reviewers to read-only access of cohort records, Programs of Study, schedule histories, clinical hour summaries, and audit trails, with no ability to create, modify, or delete any record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accreditation Reviewers require comprehensive read access for site visits without any risk of inadvertent data modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with DePaul University's Single Sign-On (SSO) identity provider using SAML 2.0 (e.g., Shibboleth), inheriting authenticated user identity and institutional role assignments from CampusConnect, and shall not maintain a separate credential store for users whose identities are managed by the university IdP.
generated_requirements.rationale: SSO integration ensures role assignments remain synchronized with authoritative university identity data and reduces credential management risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize student enrollment and demographic data from CampusConnect on a configurable schedule (default: nightly) and on-demand by a Program Administrator, detecting and surfacing discrepancies between the local record and the CampusConnect source for manual resolution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data consistency between the platform and CampusConnect is essential for accurate student tracking and FERPA-compliant record management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and document a formal interface contract (API specification or data exchange schema) for each integration point with CampusConnect and university faculty/student servers, including field mappings, data types, authentication method, and versioning strategy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit interface contracts reduce integration drift and provide a basis for failure diagnosis and change management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a fallback behavior for each external integration point such that, when CampusConnect or a university server is unavailable, the system continues to operate in a degraded read-only mode using the most recently synchronized data and displays a clearly visible staleness warning to affected users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation prevents complete operational failure when upstream university systems experience outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated email notifications to all directly affected students, instructors, and relevant Nursing Staff Members within 15 minutes of any confirmed change to a course schedule, clinical section assignment, or cohort arrangement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notification ensures stakeholders can respond to scheduling changes before they cause attendance or compliance issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configurable email notification templates for each trigger event type (e.g., schedule change, clinical site reassignment, cohort modification, clinical hour alert), allowing Program Administrators to customize subject lines, body content, and recipient rules without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable templates allow the department to tailor communications to institutional tone and specific event context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log every email notification attempt, recording the trigger event, recipient address, timestamp, delivery status (sent, bounced, failed), and any error message returned by the mail transfer agent, and shall surface delivery failures to Program Administrators within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Delivery-failure logging ensures no stakeholder misses a critical schedule change due to a silent email error.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable and PDF-exportable reports for cohorts, individual students, clinical sites, and lab sections, formatted to align with ACEN/CCNE self-study and annual report templates, including cohort progression summaries, clinical hour logs, and schedule matrices.
generated_requirements.rationale: ACEN/CCNE accreditation requires submission of standardized documentation that generic data exports cannot satisfy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to manage lab sections, including creating, editing, and deleting lab sections, assigning students and instructors to sections, and setting capacity limits per section.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lab section management is an explicitly listed administrative function required for day-to-day nursing program operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track individual student progress against their assigned Program of Study, recording course completion status, grades (pass/fail or letter grade as configured), and clinical hour totals per rotation, and shall display a progress dashboard per student accessible to Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual progress tracking is required for ACEN/CCNE student progression documentation and early intervention for at-risk students.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that no student is scheduled to a clinical site for a specialty rotation unless that site's affiliation agreement explicitly lists that specialty as an approved area, treating any attempt to do so as a hard scheduling error.
generated_requirements.rationale: Specialty-scope enforcement is a legal obligation under clinical affiliation agreements and protects both students and the university.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Illinois Nurse Practice Act minimum clinical supervision ratios as hard scheduling constraints, preventing the creation of any clinical section assignment that would exceed the maximum permissible student-to-supervisor ratio for the applicable rotation type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervision ratio requirements are statutory obligations under Illinois law and cannot be treated as advisory warnings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all cohort, schedule, student progress, clinical site, and audit trail records for a minimum of seven years following a student's graduation or program exit, in compliance with university data retention policy and ACEN/CCNE accreditation requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seven-year retention is mandated by university policy and accreditation standards for post-graduation record availability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not store, transmit, or log any patient-identifiable information. If clinical case log functionality is introduced in a future release, a formal HIPAA risk assessment and BAA review must be completed before that feature is enabled.
generated_requirements.rationale: HIPAA safeguards under 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164 apply if patient-identifiable data enters the system, so the boundary must be explicitly enforced.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a FERPA disclosure log, recording every instance in which student record data is accessed by or shared with a third party (including clinical site supervisors), capturing the recipient identity, data elements disclosed, legal basis, and timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: FERPA (20 U.S.C. § 1232g) requires institutions to maintain records of disclosures of education records to third parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to record and manage student consent or legitimate educational interest justifications required before disclosing FERPA-protected data to clinical site supervisors or other external parties.
generated_requirements.rationale: FERPA requires documented consent or a recognized exception before education records are shared with external parties such as clinical supervisors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent, and all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, for every communication channel including browser sessions, API calls to CampusConnect, and email relay connections.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of FERPA-protected student records and clinical site data at rest and in transit is a baseline security requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all Program Administrator accounts, consistent with DePaul University's institutional MFA policy, in addition to SSO-based authentication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Program Administrators have the broadest write access to sensitive records, making MFA a critical defense against credential compromise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated session after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication before any further data access or modification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout limits exposure of sensitive student and clinical data on unattended workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete any user-initiated page load or data retrieval operation within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (defined as up to 50 concurrent users), as measured at the application server.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance is essential for day-to-day administrative usability during peak scheduling periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete report generation (PDF export) for any single cohort, student, clinical site, or lab section report within 10 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accreditation reviewers and administrators require timely report generation to support planning and site visit preparation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% measured on a monthly basis, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows, to support continuous administrative operations across academic quarters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scheduling and cohort management activities occur year-round; extended downtime directly disrupts program administration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any unplanned outage and restore full service within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours, with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour of data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical scheduling data is operationally critical; loss of more than one hour of changes could cause compliance and safety issues.

generated_requirements.text: All user-facing interfaces shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act, ensuring compatibility with common assistive technologies including screen readers and keyboard-only navigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: DePaul University is a federally funded institution legally required to provide accessible technology to students and staff with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text, tooltips, and a searchable user guide accessible from every major screen, enabling new Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to complete core tasks without external training materials.
generated_requirements.rationale: Nursing department staff turnover requires that the system be learnable without reliance on institutional knowledge transfer.

generated_requirements.text: A newly onboarded Program Administrator with no prior system experience shall be able to successfully create a cohort, assign students, and schedule a course section within 30 minutes of first login, as validated by usability testing with representative users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of onboarding reduces administrative burden and ensures operational continuity during staff transitions.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a documented modular architecture with separation of concerns between the scheduling engine, notification subsystem, reporting module, and integration adapters, such that any single module can be updated or replaced without requiring changes to other modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes driven by evolving accreditation standards or university system upgrades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include automated unit and integration test suites achieving a minimum of 80% code coverage, with all tests executed as part of a CI/CD pipeline before any release is deployed to production.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated testing reduces regression risk when the scheduling logic or integration contracts are modified.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on DePaul University's existing server infrastructure (on-premises or university-managed cloud) without requiring proprietary hardware or operating system dependencies beyond those already standardized by the university's IT department.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability to existing university infrastructure avoids additional procurement costs and simplifies IT support.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all integration errors with CampusConnect and university servers — including authentication failures, schema mismatches, and timeout events — to a centralized, tamper-evident log accessible to system administrators, with each entry including a timestamp, error code, affected integration endpoint, and suggested remediation action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Detailed integration error logging accelerates diagnosis and resolution of data-consistency issues between the platform and upstream systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall re-synchronize role assignments from CampusConnect within 15 minutes of a role change being made in the university's identity provider, ensuring that revoked access is reflected in the platform without requiring a manual refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely role synchronization prevents former staff or role-changed users from retaining unauthorized access to sensitive records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent use by at least 100 authenticated users without degradation of response times below the thresholds specified in R033 and R034, as validated by load testing prior to each major release.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peak scheduling periods (e.g., quarter start) involve simultaneous use by administrators, staff, and instructors across the department.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated dashboard for Program Administrators displaying at-a-glance status indicators for: upcoming affiliation agreement expirations (within 90 days), students at risk of clinical hour shortfalls, unresolved scheduling conflicts, and pending email delivery failures.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated status dashboard reduces the cognitive load of monitoring multiple compliance and operational risk areas simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to export audit trail records in a machine-readable format (CSV and JSON) for submission to accreditation bodies or university compliance offices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable audit records support ACEN/CCNE self-study submissions and university compliance reviews without manual transcription.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data inputs (form fields, API payloads, and imported files) against defined schemas and business rules before persisting any record, returning specific, user-readable error messages that identify the offending field and the violated rule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation prevents corrupt or non-compliant data from entering the system and reduces downstream scheduling and reporting errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform and verify automated daily backups of all application data, storing backup copies in a geographically separate location from the primary server, and shall provide a documented and tested restore procedure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Daily off-site backups with a tested restore procedure are necessary to meet the RPO defined in R036 and protect irreplaceable accreditation records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, non-technical error message and log a detailed diagnostic entry whenever an external integration call fails, and shall never expose raw stack traces, internal server paths, or database schema details to end users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Suppressing technical error details from the UI prevents information leakage that could be exploited to attack the system or its integrations.

sme_subject: nursing program accreditation and clinical placement coordinator
sme_advisory: This platform sits at the intersection of FERPA-protected student records, HIPAA-adjacent clinical site data, and ACEN/CCNE nursing accreditation documentation requirements — all of which impose strict data governance obligations that a generalist requirements engineer may underestimate. The engineer should pay particular attention to the auditability of schedule changes and clinical site assignments, since accreditation bodies routinely inspect these records during site visits, and any gap in traceability can jeopardize program standing. Integration touchpoints with CampusConnect and university servers introduce significant data-consistency and authentication risks that must be addressed through well-defined interface contracts, fallback behaviors, and role-synchronization protocols.
