generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Cardmember Services Representative, Merchant Services Representative, and System Administrator — each with a precisely defined set of permitted actions (initiate, view, modify, close) on dispute cases.
generated_requirements.rationale: Distinct actor types have non-overlapping responsibilities and regulatory obligations that must be enforced at the system level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict each user's access to dispute cases exclusively within their authorized cardholder base, preventing any user from viewing or modifying cases outside their assigned portfolio; this boundary shall be enforced on every read and write operation, not only at login.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authorized cardholder base scoping is a core access-control requirement that prevents unauthorized disclosure of cardholder data and satisfies PCI DSS and internal control obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail for every dispute case, recording the actor identity, timestamp (UTC), action type, previous state, new state, and any data fields changed for every user-initiated and system-generated event; audit records shall never be deletable or modifiable by any user role including System Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: A legally mandated, examiner-reviewable audit trail is required under Reg E, Reg Z, and internal SOX controls and must be treated as a first-class functional requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and enforce a discrete case lifecycle with the following states: Initiated, Pending Documentation, Under Investigation, Provisional Credit Issued, Resolved – Favorable, Resolved – Adverse, Escalated – Pre-Arbitration, Escalated – Arbitration, Closed, and Withdrawn; only permitted state transitions shall be executable by authorized roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined state machine prevents invalid workflow transitions and ensures regulatory deadlines can be tracked against known lifecycle milestones.

generated_requirements.text: Upon creation of a dispute case, the system shall automatically calculate and store all applicable regulatory deadlines — including the Reg Z 30-day acknowledgment deadline, the Reg Z 2-billing-cycle (≤90-day) resolution deadline, the Reg E 10-business-day provisional credit deadline (5 business days for accounts open fewer than 30 days), and the Reg E 45-day (90-day for POS/foreign) final resolution deadline — based on dispute type, account type, and transaction characteristics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Statutory deadlines are hard legal obligations; automated calculation eliminates representative discretion errors and provides the basis for escalation alerting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate automated alerts to the assigned representative and their supervisor at configurable warning thresholds (default: 5 business days before each regulatory deadline) and again at breach of any statutory deadline, logging each alert as an audit event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive deadline alerting is necessary to prevent regulatory violations and supports CFPB examination readiness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a chargeback reason-code workflow engine that, upon dispute initiation, requires the user to select the applicable card network (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) and reason code; the engine shall then enforce the network-specific documentation requirements, rebuttal windows, re-presentment rights, and escalation paths (pre-arbitration, arbitration) associated with that reason code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Each card network defines distinct, reason-code-driven workflows with time-limited rights; failure to follow them results in loss of chargeback rights and potential fee liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce card-network chargeback filing windows — including Visa's 120-day window from transaction date or the date the cardholder became aware of the dispute — and shall prevent initiation of a chargeback after the applicable network deadline has passed, displaying a clear explanation to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Filing a chargeback outside the network-mandated window results in an invalid chargeback and potential fee liability; system enforcement prevents this error.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation and lifecycle management of ticket retrieval requests (merchant copy requests), tracking issuance date, merchant response deadline (default 30 days per network rules), and automatically escalating the case to a chargeback if no merchant response is received within the mandated window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Retrieval request lifecycle management and automatic escalation are required to preserve chargeback rights when merchants fail to respond.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall identify whether each disputed transaction was processed as a dual-message (authorize-and-clear) or single-message (PIN debit) transaction and shall route the dispute to the appropriate workflow, restricting available remedy types accordingly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dispute initiation rules and available remedies differ materially between dual-message and single-message transactions, and incorrect routing leads to invalid dispute filings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support issuance of provisional credit to a cardmember account by integrating with the billing system, recording the credit amount, posting date, associated case ID, and the regulatory basis (Reg E or Reg Z) in the case record and audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provisional credit issuance is a statutory obligation under both Reg E and Reg Z and must be traceable to the specific case and regulatory authority.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that provisional credit reversal is only permitted within the regulatory timeframe and only after the required advance written notice has been generated and logged; the system shall block any reversal attempt that falls outside the permitted window and shall require a distinct approval role separate from the role that issued the credit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unauthorized or untimely reversal of provisional credit violates Reg E and Reg Z and constitutes an unfair act under UDAAP; role segregation satisfies SOX internal controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role segregation for all financial adjustment actions: posting provisional credit, reversing provisional credit, and writing off a dispute loss shall each require a distinct, separately authorized role, and no single user account shall hold more than one of these roles simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: Segregation of financial adjustment duties is required by SOX internal controls and reduces the risk of fraud or error in dispute-related financial postings.

generated_requirements.text: For credit card (Reg Z) disputes, the system shall automatically generate a written acknowledgment letter to the cardholder within 30 calendar days of dispute receipt, referencing the specific billing error category under 12 CFR 1026.13, and shall record the generation timestamp and delivery method in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: 12 CFR 1026.13 mandates a 30-day written acknowledgment; automated generation ensures compliance and creates an auditable record.

generated_requirements.text: For credit card (Reg Z) disputes, the system shall generate a written explanation of findings within 2 billing cycles of receiving the dispute (not to exceed 90 days), including the specific billing error category, the outcome, and, for adverse determinations, the specific reasons and the cardholder's right to request documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: 12 CFR 1026.13 requires a written explanation of findings within 2 billing cycles; failure to comply exposes the institution to regulatory liability.

generated_requirements.text: For EFT/debit (Reg E) disputes, the system shall generate a written notice of findings — both favorable and adverse — within the statutory timeframe, and for adverse determinations the notice shall include the specific statement of reasons required by 12 CFR 1005.11(d), the cardholder's right to request documents used in the investigation, and the telephone number for further inquiry.
generated_requirements.rationale: 12 CFR 1005.11(d) mandates specific content in adverse-finding notices; the system must generate compliant notices to avoid regulatory violations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prohibit any financial action that collects, offsets, or restricts the disputed amount from the cardmember's account during the investigation period for Reg Z disputes, and shall enforce this prohibition automatically without relying on representative discretion.
generated_requirements.rationale: 12 CFR 1026.13 explicitly prohibits collection of disputed amounts during investigation; system-level enforcement prevents inadvertent UDAAP violations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the cardmember database to retrieve account holder information (name, account number, account open date, account type, billing cycle dates) at dispute initiation and shall surface data retrieval failures to the user with a clear error message, preventing case creation until account data is successfully validated.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate account data is required to correctly calculate regulatory deadlines and determine applicable dispute rules; integration failures must not result in silently incorrect case data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the merchant database to retrieve merchant information (name, MID, acquiring bank, network) when creating chargeback notifications or retrieval requests, and shall log all data retrieved from the merchant database in the case record at the time of retrieval.
generated_requirements.rationale: Merchant data is required for correct chargeback routing and network compliance; point-in-time logging preserves the data state used for each decision.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the billing system to post provisional credits, reverse credits, and record write-offs; all billing system transactions shall be idempotent (duplicate submissions shall not result in duplicate postings), and the system shall reconcile each posting confirmation against the case record within 1 business day.
generated_requirements.rationale: Idempotent billing integration prevents duplicate financial postings that would harm cardmembers and create reconciliation failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the letter printing utility to generate, queue, and confirm delivery of all regulatory correspondence (Reg E notices, Reg Z acknowledgments and explanations, adverse-action notices); if the letter printing utility is unavailable, the system shall queue correspondence and retry delivery, alerting the assigned representative if delivery is not confirmed within 24 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely delivery of regulatory correspondence is legally mandated; queuing and retry logic ensures deadlines are not missed due to transient integration failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to search for existing dispute cases using at least the following criteria: account number (masked PAN), cardholder name, transaction date range, dispute case ID, dispute status, assigned representative, and card network; search results shall be filtered to the user's authorized cardholder base.
generated_requirements.rationale: Efficient case search is essential for representative productivity and for supervisory oversight, while cardholder-base filtering enforces access control on search results.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support flagging a dispute case for potential first-party fraud or suspicious activity, triggering a referral workflow to the BSA/AML compliance team without displaying any tip-off information to the subject cardholder or the representative who initiated the flag; the flag and referral shall be recorded in the audit trail with restricted visibility.
generated_requirements.rationale: BSA/AML obligations require SAR referral workflows that do not tip off subjects; restricted audit visibility protects the integrity of the referral process.

generated_requirements.text: If a dispute resolution results in a credit reporting correction, the system shall initiate a furnisher dispute investigation workflow, generate the required correction report to consumer reporting agencies, and record the submission date and agency confirmation within the case record to demonstrate compliance within the 30-day FCRA/FACTA deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: FCRA/FACTA requires furnishers to investigate and correct inaccurate credit reporting within 30 days; the system must support and document this workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent re-aging of dispute cases and shall enforce card-network presentment time limits by calculating and displaying the remaining filing window at all times; any attempt to file a chargeback or re-presentment outside the permitted window shall be blocked with a logged rejection reason.
generated_requirements.rationale: Re-aging and out-of-window filings violate card network operating regulations and can result in invalid chargebacks and fee liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dispute initiation workflow that does not impose any barriers beyond identity verification and account validation; it shall not require cardmembers or representatives to submit documentation as a precondition for opening a case, and it shall not automatically deny any dispute without a completed investigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: UDAAP prohibits creating barriers to dispute initiation or automatically denying disputes without investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not impose any fee on a cardmember related to the dispute process unless the fee is disclosed in the cardmember agreement and is permitted under applicable regulations; any fee assessment shall require explicit System Administrator authorization and shall be recorded in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: UDAAP prohibits undisclosed fees related to dispute processing; explicit authorization and audit logging provide a defensible compliance record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall mask Primary Account Numbers (PANs) in all UI displays and audit log entries, showing only the last four digits; CVV values and full expiry dates shall never be stored or displayed within the dispute application.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 requires PAN masking in displays and prohibits storage of CVV and sensitive authentication data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall export audit trail records and case data in structured, machine-readable formats (at minimum CSV and JSON) to support CFPB examination requests; exports shall be filterable by date range, case type, representative, and regulatory basis, and shall be executable only by System Administrator role.
generated_requirements.rationale: CFPB examination readiness requires structured, exportable audit and case data; restricting export to System Administrator prevents unauthorized data exfiltration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all dispute case records, audit trail entries, and generated correspondence for a minimum of 7 years from case closure, with the first 2 years of records being immediately accessible online and older records accessible within 24 hours from archive.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reg E and Reg Z mandate minimum 2-year retention; institutional policy and examination risk typically require 7 years, with tiered access reflecting operational vs. archival needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit all cardholder data between the browser client, application server, and back-end integration endpoints exclusively over TLS 1.2 or higher; unencrypted transmission of cardholder data shall be technically prevented, not merely prohibited by policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 4 mandates encryption of cardholder data in transit; technical enforcement is stronger than policy-only controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user logins; sessions shall time out after no more than 15 minutes of inactivity, and re-authentication shall be required before any financial adjustment action regardless of session age.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 and internal access-control requirements mandate MFA and session controls to prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply the principle of least privilege to all service accounts used for back-end integrations (cardmember DB, merchant DB, billing system, letter utility); each service account shall have only the permissions required for its specific integration function and shall be reviewed quarterly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege service accounts limit the blast radius of a compromised integration credential and satisfy PCI DSS access-control requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to dispute case search queries within 3 seconds at the 95th percentile under a concurrent load of 500 active users, and shall complete dispute case creation (including all synchronous back-end integration calls) within 10 seconds at the 95th percentile.
generated_requirements.rationale: Representative productivity and regulatory timeliness depend on responsive system performance; defined SLOs provide a measurable acceptance criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process automated deadline calculations, alert generation, and state-transition triggers for up to 100,000 open dispute cases concurrently without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance SLOs.
generated_requirements.rationale: At scale, background processing of deadline and alert logic must not compete with interactive user operations or cause missed regulatory notifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (measured monthly, excluding pre-approved maintenance windows), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside business hours and communicated to users at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory deadlines are calendar-driven and do not pause for system downtime; high availability is required to ensure representatives can act within statutory windows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement graceful degradation for each back-end integration: if the cardmember database, merchant database, billing system, or letter utility is unavailable, the system shall queue dependent operations, surface a clear status indicator to the user, and resume processing automatically upon restoration without data loss or duplicate actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration failures must not result in missed regulatory deadlines, duplicate financial postings, or silent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated reconciliation checks between dispute case financial records and billing system postings on a daily basis, generating an exception report for any discrepancy and alerting the System Administrator role within 1 hour of detection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial data integrity between the dispute system and billing system is critical for regulatory compliance and accurate cardholder account management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard enterprise web infrastructure (Linux-based application servers, relational database, load balancer) and shall not require proprietary hardware or operating system dependencies that would prevent migration between hosting environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Avoiding proprietary infrastructure dependencies reduces vendor lock-in and supports business continuity planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system's integration layer shall use documented, versioned APIs for all back-end system connections; breaking changes to integration contracts shall require a version increment and a parallel-run period of at least 30 days before the prior version is retired.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned integration APIs allow back-end systems to evolve independently without causing unplanned outages in the dispute application.

generated_requirements.text: The chargeback reason-code workflow engine shall be configurable by System Administrators via a rules management interface without requiring code deployment, enabling updates to network reason codes, documentation requirements, and deadline windows within 5 business days of a card network rule change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Card network operating regulations change periodically; configuration-driven rules management allows timely compliance updates without software release cycles.

generated_requirements.text: All regulatory deadline parameters (Reg E provisional credit windows, Reg Z billing cycle calculations, network chargeback filing windows) shall be stored as configurable system parameters rather than hard-coded values, with changes requiring System Administrator authorization and generating an audit log entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory parameters are subject to amendment; externalized configuration with audit logging enables compliant updates without code changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and shall be operable in current versions of Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance supports inclusive use by internal and external representatives, and cross-browser compatibility reduces support burden.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display all applicable regulatory deadlines, remaining filing windows, and case status prominently on the case detail screen, using visual indicators (color coding and countdown timers) that escalate in urgency as deadlines approach, to minimize the risk of representative oversight.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prominent deadline visibility reduces the likelihood of regulatory breaches caused by representative inattention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data entered during dispute initiation and case updates against defined business rules (e.g., transaction date not in the future, dispute amount not exceeding transaction amount, reason code compatible with card network) before persisting any record, returning specific field-level error messages for each validation failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation at the point of entry prevents invalid case data that could lead to incorrect regulatory deadline calculations or invalid chargeback filings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent editing protection on dispute cases: if two users attempt to modify the same case simultaneously, the second user's save attempt shall be rejected with a conflict notification, and no partial or merged updates shall be silently persisted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrent edit conflicts on financial and regulatory records can produce data corruption; optimistic locking prevents silent data integrity failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a supervisor dashboard displaying all open cases assigned to their team, filterable by deadline proximity, case status, and regulatory basis, with the ability to reassign cases between representatives within the same authorized cardholder base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts, privilege escalation attempts, and access-control violations in a separate security event log that is write-only for all application roles and is forwarded to the institution's SIEM in real time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo a PCI DSS-scoped penetration test at least annually and after any significant infrastructure change, with critical and high findings remediated within 30 days and evidence of remediation retained for examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a recovery point objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour and a recovery time objective (RTO) of no more than 4 hours for all dispute case data and audit trail records, with backup and recovery procedures tested at least semi-annually.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory deadlines and financial records require stringent data recovery guarantees; tested RPO/RTO targets ensure business continuity after a system failure.

domain: banking / financial services – credit card dispute management
sme_subject: credit card disputes and chargebacks compliance officer (Reg E / Reg Z / Card Network Operating Regulations)
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of federal consumer protection law (Reg E for debit/prepaid, Reg Z for open-end credit), card network operating regulations (Visa/Mastercard/Amex chargeback rules), and internal operational controls. Requirements must be precise about statutory deadlines, reason-code-driven workflows, and financial adjustment reversibility — areas where a generalist engineer is likely to under-specify. The audit trail and role-based access requirements are not merely good practice; they are legally mandated and subject to examiner review, so they must be treated as first-class functional requirements rather than non-functional afterthoughts. Integration touchpoints (billing system, letter utility, card network back-end) carry significant data-integrity and timing risk that must be explicitly addressed in both functional and reliability requirements.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall comply with the corporate standards for user interface creation for internally and externally used applications.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Cardmember Information Database. ground_truth_requirements.text: All authorized users once authenticated must be able to logon to the Disputes System

ground_truth_requirements.text: All additions of new users and modifications to user's access shall be recorded on the User Report.

ground_truth_requirements.text: All business rules specified in the Disputes System shall be in compliance to the guidelines of Regulation E and Regulation Z.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must maintain a detailed history of every action that a user takes on a dispute case. This ensures a complete audit trail if questions arise later on with regard to a particular dispute case.

ground_truth_requirements.text: All actions that modify an existing dispute case must be recorded in the case history.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must be accessible by both internal and external users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent users from accessing any dispute cases that do not belong to their cardholder base.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide different levels of access with regard to disputes case initiation and follow-up actions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the users to select disputable transactions (based on the age of the transaction) from a user interface and initiate a dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) on the selected transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the user the ability to initiate a single dispute case on multiple transactions that belong to a single merchant.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the user to create three unique types of ticket retrieval requests. The three types of ticket retrieval requests are (1) Request for original receipt (2) Request for a copy of the receipt or (3) Request for a portfolio. A portfolio consists of documentation that would provide proof of a purchase such as the documentation that is received from a car rental agency that is more than a sales receipt.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent external users from requesting original receipts. Requests for original receipts are restricted to internal users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of ticket retrieval request that contains the following information; the dispute case number the type of retrieval requested (copy original or portfolio) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall allow the user to create or initiate a chargeback request. The system must prompt the user to enter the required information to initiate a chargeback request. The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number (2) the merchant account number (3) the cardmember account number and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide collision estimators with the ability to search the ChoiceParts external data feed for available recycled parts by part type, part number, vehicle make/model/year, and supplier, returning results that include part grade (e.g., ARA grade, CAPA/NSF certification status), price, and availability status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimators must be able to locate and evaluate recycled parts from ChoiceParts as the primary sourcing workflow for collision estimates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to add recycled parts sourced from ChoiceParts to a collision estimate, automatically populating part grade, supplier, price, and availability confirmation status on the relevant estimate line item.
generated_requirements.rationale: Applying recycled parts to estimates is the core estimator workflow and must capture all data needed for valuation and compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce insurer-specific recycled parts substitution rules, including minimum part grade thresholds (e.g., CAPA or NSF certification equivalency), vehicle age and mileage eligibility windows, and restrictions prohibiting recycled parts on structural components, preventing non-compliant parts from being added to an estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Carrier-specific substitution policies are a contractual and regulatory obligation that must be enforced at the point of parts selection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate a hold or reservation request with ChoiceParts when a recycled part is added to an estimate, and shall prominently flag any part whose availability has not been confirmed or whose reservation has expired on the estimate view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Recycled parts inventory is volatile; unconfirmed availability must be surfaced to prevent estimate inaccuracies and claim settlement errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compare the recycled part price retrieved from ChoiceParts against the OEM list price for the equivalent part and apply betterment and depreciation calculations per the applicable carrier policy, displaying both the raw and adjusted prices on the estimate line item.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate parts pricing and valuation directly affect claim settlement amounts and must reflect carrier-specific depreciation rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise recycled parts inventory data from the ChoiceParts external data feed at a configurable interval not exceeding 15 minutes during business hours, and shall timestamp each data refresh to indicate data freshness to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Frequent synchronisation ensures estimators work with current inventory data, reducing the risk of selecting unavailable parts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect failures in the ChoiceParts data feed connection, notify affected users with a clearly visible alert indicating that parts data may be stale, log the failure event with timestamp and error details, and automatically retry the connection at defined intervals until restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration failures must be handled gracefully to maintain estimator productivity and provide an auditable record of data feed outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete version history of each collision estimate, recording every revision (supplement) with a timestamp, the identity of the authoring user, a mandatory reason code selected from a configurable list, and a diff of changed line items.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimate supplements are a routine part of collision repair workflows and must be fully traceable for audit, compliance, and dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage each claim and estimate through a defined status lifecycle — Draft, Submitted, Under Review, Approved, Supplement Requested, and Closed — enforcing role-gated transitions so that only authorised user roles may advance or revert a status, and recording the timestamp and acting user for every transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined status machine with role-gated transitions is required for accurate SLA tracking, downstream payment processing, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to assign a preferred repair facility to a claim, restricting the selectable facilities to those on the applicable carrier's Direct Repair Program (DRP) network, and shall record the assignment with a timestamp and the assigning adjuster's identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: DRP network enforcement ensures carriers' contracted labor rates and parts sourcing agreements are honoured during facility assignment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to review completed collision estimates, add review notes, and approve or reject the estimate with a mandatory disposition reason, triggering the appropriate status transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjuster review and approval is a core workflow step that gates claim settlement and must be fully documented.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to request an audit report for one or more claims or estimates, specifying the scope (e.g., by estimator, repair facility, date range, or claim cohort) and triggering report generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjusters need on-demand audit reports to evaluate recycled parts utilisation and support internal KPI and regulatory reporting obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate audit reports that include recycled parts usage rates broken down by estimator, repair facility, and claim cohort, with drill-down capability to individual estimate line items, and shall export reports in at least PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable, drill-down audit reports are required to satisfy both internal KPIs and potential state regulatory reporting requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to assign a performance rating to a repair facility following an audit event, requiring the rating to be linked to a specific audit report, accompanied by supporting evidence notes, and stored immutably once submitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Facility ratings must be audit-linked and immutable to ensure they are evidence-based and legally defensible.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall incorporate repair facility performance ratings into the facility assignment recommendation logic, surfacing higher-rated DRP facilities preferentially when an adjuster assigns a repair facility to a claim.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ratings must feed back into assignment workflows to fulfil the business objective of guiding future facility selections based on audit outcomes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Collision Estimator, Adjuster, and Supervisory Adjuster — ensuring each role can access only the functions and data explicitly permitted for that role, and that no role can escalate its own privileges.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precise role boundaries are required to reflect real-world adjuster hierarchy, licensing distinctions, and regulatory access control obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and display the adjuster license number, issuing state, and expiration date for each adjuster user account, and shall warn administrators and the adjuster when a license is within 30 days of expiration or has expired, preventing expired-license adjusters from approving claims.
generated_requirements.rationale: Many jurisdictions require adjusters to hold a valid state license; the system must prevent unlicensed individuals from approving claims.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a written recycled/non-OEM parts disclosure document as part of the estimate output whenever a recycled or aftermarket part is included, identifying each such part by description and line item, suitable for delivery to the vehicle owner in compliance with applicable state disclosure obligations.
generated_requirements.rationale: State laws (e.g., Illinois, Texas) mandate written disclosure to vehicle owners when non-OEM or recycled parts are used in a repair.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track configurable SLA deadlines for each claim — including claim acknowledgment, investigation completion, estimate turnaround, and supplement approval windows — display countdown indicators to responsible users, and generate alerts when a deadline is at risk or breached.
generated_requirements.rationale: State Fair Claims Settlement Practices Acts and DRP contracts impose mandatory timeframes that the system must actively support and monitor.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce SLA compliance reports per carrier, showing the percentage of claims meeting each configured deadline, with drill-down to individual breached claims, to support regulatory examination and DRP contract reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: NAIC Model Laws and carrier SLA obligations require demonstrable, reportable compliance against defined turnaround commitments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a legal hold flag on any claim or estimate record that, when set by an authorised administrator, prevents deletion, modification, or archival of that record until the hold is explicitly released by an authorised user, with all hold and release actions logged immutably.
generated_requirements.rationale: Insurance claim records subject to litigation or regulatory examination must be preserved in a non-alterable format per state data retention laws.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all claim, estimate, supplement, audit report, and facility rating records for a minimum of seven years from the date of claim closure, after which records may be archived but must remain retrievable within 48 hours upon request.
generated_requirements.rationale: State-mandated retention periods of 5–7 years require the system to preserve records and ensure they remain accessible for regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-tenancy by logically and physically isolating each insurance carrier's claim data, configuration, and user accounts so that no user, query, or process belonging to one carrier can access or infer data belonging to another carrier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Carriers are contractually and regulatorily required to ensure their policyholders' claim data is not accessible to competing carriers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow each insurance carrier tenant to independently configure labor rate schedules, parts sourcing preferences, recycled parts substitution thresholds, DRP network membership, audit report thresholds, SLA deadlines, and branding without any configuration change affecting other tenants.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-carrier SaaS deployments require per-tenant configuration independence to support diverse carrier policies and contractual obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit log of all user actions — including logins, data access, estimate creation and modification, status transitions, facility assignments, ratings, and configuration changes — recording the user identity, tenant, timestamp, action type, and affected record identifiers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit logging is required for regulatory examination, fraud detection, and demonstrating data isolation compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display all recycled parts on an estimate with their ARA grade or applicable certification (CAPA, NSF) prominently labelled on both the internal estimate view and any printed or exported estimate document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Parts grading standards compliance must be documented on the estimate to satisfy repairability, safety, and regulatory requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support management of carrier-specific preferred supplier lists, allowing carrier administrators to add, remove, and rank suppliers, and shall restrict estimators to searching only suppliers on their carrier's preferred list unless explicitly overridden by an authorised role.
generated_requirements.rationale: DRP parts sourcing agreements require that estimators use carrier-contracted suppliers to ensure negotiated pricing and quality standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide collision estimators with a dashboard showing their open estimates, pending parts reservations, approaching SLA deadlines, and ChoiceParts feed status, accessible immediately upon login.
generated_requirements.rationale: A role-specific dashboard improves estimator productivity by surfacing the most time-sensitive information at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide adjusters with a dashboard showing claims awaiting review, SLA deadline countdowns, pending facility assignments, and requested audit report statuses.
generated_requirements.rationale: An adjuster-specific dashboard supports timely claim handling and SLA compliance by prioritising actionable items.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide supervisory adjusters with a dashboard showing audit report outcomes, facility rating history, recycled parts utilisation trends across their carrier's claims, and estimator performance summaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervisory adjusters require aggregated performance visibility to fulfil their oversight and quality assurance responsibilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to all interactive user requests — including parts searches, estimate loading, and status transitions — within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 500 concurrent users per tenant).
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance is critical in both office and repair shop environments where estimators and adjusters work under time pressure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate audit reports containing up to 10,000 claim records within 30 seconds of the request being submitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely report generation is necessary for adjusters and supervisory adjusters to act on audit findings without workflow disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-tenant data isolation at both the application layer (query scoping by tenant identifier) and the data layer (row-level security or schema-per-tenant), and shall provide a documented, independently verifiable isolation architecture that can be presented to state insurance regulators upon request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Demonstrable and auditable data isolation is a regulatory and contractual requirement for carriers sharing a multi-tenant platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all claim and estimate data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent, and all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, with encryption keys managed per tenant and rotated at least annually.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of sensitive insurance claim data at rest and in transit is a baseline security requirement for regulatory compliance and carrier trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via multi-factor authentication (MFA) and shall support integration with carrier-provided identity providers via SAML 2.0 or OpenID Connect, enforcing session timeouts of no more than 30 minutes of inactivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication and SSO integration are required to protect sensitive claims data and align with carrier enterprise security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% measured monthly, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows, and shall support planned maintenance during off-peak hours with advance notice to all affected tenants.
generated_requirements.rationale: Claims processing is time-sensitive and subject to regulatory deadlines; system downtime directly risks SLA and compliance breaches.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour, with automated failover to a geographically redundant environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Insurance claims workflows cannot tolerate extended data loss or downtime given regulatory deadline obligations and financial settlement impacts.

generated_requirements.text: The ChoiceParts integration component shall implement automatic retry with exponential backoff on transient failures, circuit-breaker logic to prevent cascading failures, and shall degrade gracefully by allowing estimators to manually enter parts data when the feed is unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: The ChoiceParts feed is a critical external dependency; resilient integration patterns are required to maintain estimator productivity during outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and shall be operable on current versions of major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) without requiring browser plugins, supporting use on both desktop workstations and tablet devices.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system must be accessible in both office and repair shop environments across diverse hardware and must meet accessibility obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable a new collision estimator with basic insurance claims knowledge to complete a recycled parts search and apply a part to an estimate without formal training, as validated by usability testing with a task completion rate of at least 85% on first attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use in the core estimator workflow reduces training costs and errors in repair shop environments where turnover may be high.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected to allow new insurance carrier tenants to be onboarded — including configuration, user provisioning, and DRP network setup — within 5 business days, without requiring code changes or system downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalable tenant onboarding is essential for a multi-carrier SaaS platform to grow its customer base efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all integration points — including the ChoiceParts feed, identity providers, and report exports — through versioned APIs, ensuring that updates to internal components do not break existing integrations without a defined deprecation notice period of at least 90 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned APIs protect external integrations from breaking changes and support long-term platform stability for carrier and partner integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on major cloud infrastructure providers (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) without modification to application code, using containerised deployment (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to support portability across environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud-agnostic deployment ensures the platform is not locked to a single provider and can meet carrier-specific hosting requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all ChoiceParts data feed synchronisation events — including start time, end time, record counts, data freshness timestamp, and any errors — and make these logs accessible to system administrators for monitoring and troubleshooting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Detailed integration logs are essential for diagnosing data freshness issues and demonstrating feed reliability to carriers and regulators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent any single tenant's workload — including bulk report generation or high-volume parts searches — from degrading response times for other tenants, enforcing per-tenant resource quotas and rate limiting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Noisy-neighbour effects in a multi-tenant environment could cause SLA breaches for unrelated carriers and must be actively prevented.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all inputs — including parts search parameters, estimate line item values, and configuration fields — against defined schemas server-side, rejecting malformed or out-of-range values with descriptive error messages, to prevent injection attacks and data corruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Server-side input validation is a foundational security control required to protect against injection vulnerabilities and data integrity issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo a third-party penetration test at least annually and after any major release, with findings remediated according to a risk-tiered schedule (Critical within 7 days, High within 30 days, Medium within 90 days), and remediation evidence retained for regulatory review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular penetration testing and timely remediation are required to maintain the security posture expected by insurance carrier clients and regulators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide carrier administrators with a self-service tenant configuration portal allowing them to manage user accounts, roles, DRP networks, labor rate schedules, parts sourcing rules, and SLA thresholds, with all configuration changes logged and reversible via a configuration history view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-service configuration management reduces operational dependency on the platform vendor and empowers carriers to adapt quickly to policy changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the relevant adjuster and their supervisor via in-application alert and email when a claim's SLA deadline has been breached, including the claim identifier, breached deadline type, and elapsed overage time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive breach notifications are required to enable corrective action and to document the carrier's awareness of compliance failures for regulatory purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce a data isolation compliance report on demand for each tenant, listing all data access events for that tenant's records within a specified date range, exportable in a format suitable for submission to state insurance regulators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Carriers must be able to demonstrate to regulators that their policyholders' data has not been accessed by other tenants, requiring an auditable access report.

sme_subject: automotive collision repair and insurance claims adjuster
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of insurance claims compliance, automotive repair industry workflows, and multi-tenant SaaS architecture. Requirements engineers should pay close attention to the regulatory obligations governing insurance claims handling (including state-level fair claims settlement practices acts), the nuanced business rules around recycled/OEM/aftermarket parts usage and their impact on claim valuation, and the strict data isolation demands of serving multiple insurance carriers on a shared platform. The ChoiceParts integration is a critical external dependency whose reliability directly affects estimator productivity and claim accuracy, so integration failure modes and data freshness requirements deserve explicit treatment. Role-based access must reflect real-world adjuster hierarchy and licensing distinctions, not just generic permission tiers.
