generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Employee, Meeting Organizer, and Facilities/Building Manager — each with explicitly scoped permissions over rooms, equipment, reports, and user data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Distinct permission boundaries per role prevent unauthorized access to sensitive scheduling, reporting, and facilities data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to search for available conference rooms by date, time range, building, floor, capacity, and accessibility attributes (e.g., wheelchair access, hearing loop, AV equipment).
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive search filters enable employees to find rooms that meet both logistical and accessibility needs efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent double-booking of any conference room by enforcing real-time concurrency controls, ensuring that only one confirmed reservation can exist for a given room at any overlapping time interval.
generated_requirements.rationale: Double-booking prevention is a core correctness requirement for any room reservation system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to create, edit, and cancel meeting reservations, attach an agenda, and invite internal and external attendees by name or email address.
generated_requirements.rationale: Meeting organizers require full lifecycle management of meetings including agenda and attendee coordination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support recurring meeting series with configurable recurrence patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, custom), and shall allow a meeting organizer to skip, modify, or cancel a single occurrence without affecting the remainder of the series.
generated_requirements.rationale: Recurring meeting exception handling reflects real-world scheduling needs and prevents unnecessary series cancellations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send meeting invitations to attendees via email and display in-app notifications, and shall allow attendees to accept, decline, or propose an alternative time directly from the invitation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Invitation response workflows are essential for coordinating attendee availability and confirming meeting participation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement an automated room-release ('no-show') policy whereby, if a meeting organizer or designated attendee does not check in within a configurable grace period (default: 15 minutes) after the scheduled start time, the room reservation is automatically cancelled and the room is returned to the available pool.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated no-show release maximizes room utilization by reclaiming unused reservations without manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a check-in mechanism (via web UI, QR code scan, or room panel integration) that meeting organizers or attendees can use to confirm room occupancy and prevent automatic room release.
generated_requirements.rationale: A check-in mechanism is required to support the no-show policy and accurately track actual room usage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees and meeting organizers to reserve equipment items (e.g., projectors, video conferencing units, whiteboards) in conjunction with or independently of a room reservation, and shall track each equipment item's availability, delivery status, and return status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment reservation and lifecycle tracking ensures that required resources are available and accounted for at meeting time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support equipment transport requests, allowing a requester to specify a delivery location and time, and shall provide facilities staff with a task queue showing pending, in-transit, and completed transport assignments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transport logistics tracking bridges the gap between equipment reservation and physical delivery, reducing operational failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display interactive building floor-plan maps with real-time room availability overlays, allowing users to visually identify available, occupied, and reserved rooms on each floor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual floor-plan maps improve spatial awareness and accelerate room selection for users unfamiliar with building layouts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose accessibility attributes for each room (e.g., wheelchair-accessible entrance, hearing loop, adjustable-height furniture, accessible restroom proximity) and allow users to filter room search results by these attributes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility attribute filtering is required to comply with ADA and EN 301 549 obligations and to serve employees with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable maximum room capacity limits per room, prevent bookings that exceed the configured capacity, and record the declared occupancy for each reservation for facilities compliance reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Capacity enforcement satisfies fire safety and occupancy regulations and protects the organization from compliance liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send configurable reminders and notifications to meeting organizers and attendees via email and in-app channels at user-defined intervals before a meeting (e.g., 24 hours, 1 hour, 15 minutes), with optional SMS or push notification channels.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely reminders reduce no-shows and ensure attendees are prepared, improving overall meeting effectiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support escalation notification workflows for unconfirmed bookings, automatically notifying the meeting organizer and, after a configurable escalation period, their designated manager if a booking remains unconfirmed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Escalation paths prevent ghost reservations from blocking room availability due to organizer inaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Microsoft Exchange/Outlook and Google Workspace calendar platforms, enabling two-way synchronization of meeting events, room resource calendars, and attendee availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Calendar integration ensures the system fits into existing enterprise workflows without requiring users to maintain separate schedules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with enterprise LDAP and Microsoft Active Directory services for user provisioning, authentication, and single sign-on (SSO), supporting SAML 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directory integration enables centralized user management and eliminates the need for separate credential management within the application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-timezone scheduling, displaying meeting times in each user's configured local timezone and each room's local timezone, and shall correctly handle daylight saving time transitions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-timezone support is essential for globally distributed organizations to avoid scheduling errors across time zones.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with reservation reports that include booking history, room utilization rates, no-show rates, equipment usage, and cost-center/chargeback data, exportable in CSV, PDF, and Excel formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting capabilities enable managers to optimize resource allocation and support internal chargeback and compliance processes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail recording every create, read (for sensitive data), update, and delete action on reservations, user accounts, room configurations, and equipment records, including the actor identity, timestamp, and before/after values.
generated_requirements.rationale: A full audit trail is required for SOC 2 Type II / ISO 27001 compliance and supports forensic investigation of scheduling disputes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a waitlist mechanism whereby, when a requested room is fully booked, a user may join a waitlist and be automatically notified and offered the reservation if a cancellation occurs within a configurable window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Waitlist management improves room utilization and provides a fair, automated conflict resolution path for high-demand rooms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Facilities/Building Managers to override any existing reservation (with mandatory justification entry), notify affected parties automatically, and reassign the room or offer alternative rooms to displaced meeting organizers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manager override capability is required for emergency situations, VIP events, or facilities maintenance that supersede standard bookings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide multiple calendar views for meeting schedules — including daily, weekly, monthly, and agenda list views — filterable by building, floor, room, organizer, and cost center.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple views accommodate different user workflows and allow managers to monitor scheduling across organizational dimensions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-building and multi-site deployments, allowing administrators to define buildings, floors, rooms, and equipment within a hierarchical organizational structure, and allowing users to search and book across all sites from a single interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-site support is required for organizations with offices across multiple locations or countries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully configurable and brandable per customer organization, supporting custom logos, color schemes, terminology, and domain names without requiring source code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Branding and configurability are required for enterprise customers who need the system to reflect their corporate identity and terminology.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multilingual user interfaces with at minimum English, French, German, Spanish, and Simplified Chinese, with the ability to add additional languages via externalized resource bundles without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multilingual support is required for global deployments and ensures usability for non-English-speaking employees.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide administrators with configurable data retention policies per data category (e.g., reservation records, attendee lists, audit logs), and shall automatically purge or anonymize personal data upon policy expiry in compliance with GDPR and CCPA.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated data retention and purge workflows are required to comply with GDPR Article 5(1)(e) and CCPA data minimization obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a data subject rights management interface allowing administrators to fulfill GDPR/CCPA requests for data access, data portability, and right-to-erasure within a configurable SLA (default: 30 days).
generated_requirements.rationale: Data subject rights tooling is a legal obligation under GDPR Article 15–17 and CCPA Section 1798.100–1798.125.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configurable data residency settings, allowing administrators to designate the geographic region(s) in which meeting, personnel, and audit data are stored and replicated, and shall prevent data from being written outside designated regions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data residency controls are required to satisfy corporate and regulatory mandates restricting cross-border transfer of employee personal data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with standard SMTP-compliant email servers and shall support TLS-enforced email delivery and optionally S/MIME signing/encryption for meeting invitation emails, configurable per organizational policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure email integration ensures meeting invitation confidentiality and compliance with organizational email relay policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support standard relational database management systems (at minimum Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, and PostgreSQL) via a configurable database abstraction layer, without requiring application code changes to switch between supported DBMS platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: DBMS portability allows customers to use their existing enterprise database infrastructure and reduces vendor lock-in.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sustain a minimum of 5,000 concurrent authenticated user sessions without degradation of response times below the thresholds defined in R033, as verified by load testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large enterprise deployments require the system to handle thousands of simultaneous users without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results for room availability queries within 2 seconds at the 95th percentile under normal load (up to 2,000 concurrent users), and within 5 seconds at the 95th percentile under peak load (up to 5,000 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Measurable response time thresholds ensure the system remains usable under realistic enterprise load conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% measured monthly, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows, and shall support rolling deployments that allow updates to be applied without full system downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is critical for a scheduling system that employees depend on throughout the business day across global time zones.

generated_requirements.text: The system's web user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, including keyboard navigability, sufficient color contrast ratios, screen-reader compatibility (ARIA landmarks), and accessible form labeling.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA conformance is required by ADA and EN 301 549 and ensures the system is usable by employees with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be operable via any modern evergreen web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari — latest two major versions) without requiring browser plugins, extensions, or client-side software installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser-only access eliminates deployment friction and ensures the system is accessible from any corporate or personal device.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all personally identifiable information and sensitive reservation data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent, with encryption keys managed via a dedicated key management service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest and in transit is a baseline control required by SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and GDPR Article 32.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrative and manager-level accounts, and shall support MFA for standard user accounts as a configurable organizational policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access to privileged accounts and sensitive scheduling data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication events (successful login, failed login, logout, session timeout, MFA challenge) and all privileged administrative actions to a tamper-evident audit log, retaining logs for a minimum of 12 months or as configured by the administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication and administrative action logging is required for SOC 2 Type II audit evidence and incident response investigations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented in Java (minimum Java 17 LTS) using a modular, layered architecture that separates presentation, business logic, and data access concerns, enabling independent testing and replacement of each layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular Java architecture reduces the cost of long-term maintenance and enables targeted updates without system-wide regression risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a comprehensive administrator configuration interface (UI and API) for managing buildings, rooms, equipment, user roles, notification templates, branding assets, and retention policies without requiring application redeployment or code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Runtime configurability minimizes administrative overhead and reduces dependency on development teams for routine operational changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a versioned RESTful API (OpenAPI 3.0 documented) for all core functions — room search, reservation management, equipment tracking, user management, and reporting — to support third-party integrations and custom client development.
generated_requirements.rationale: A documented public API enables integration with other enterprise systems and future extensibility without modifying core application code.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on-premises, in a private cloud, or in a public cloud environment (AWS, Azure, GCP), and shall provide deployment artifacts (container images, Helm charts, or equivalent) that support automated CI/CD pipeline integration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible deployment options accommodate diverse enterprise infrastructure strategies and data residency requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include an automated suite of unit, integration, and end-to-end regression tests achieving a minimum of 80% code coverage, executable as part of a CI/CD pipeline, with test results published in a standard format (e.g., JUnit XML).
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated test coverage ensures that changes introduced during the multi-year lifecycle do not silently break existing functionality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement protection against the OWASP Top 10 web application vulnerabilities (current edition), including SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), insecure deserialization, and broken access control, verified by annual penetration testing.
generated_requirements.rationale: OWASP Top 10 mitigation is a baseline security requirement for enterprise web applications and is commonly mandated by procurement security reviews.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a new employee user with the ability to complete a room search, create a reservation, and send meeting invitations within no more than 5 minutes without requiring training, as validated by usability testing with representative users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low time-to-productivity for new users reduces training costs and drives adoption across large employee populations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support horizontal scaling of application server nodes behind a load balancer, with session state managed externally (e.g., distributed cache) so that any node can serve any authenticated user request without sticky sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Stateless horizontal scaling enables the system to handle traffic spikes and supports zero-downtime rolling deployments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a health-check and monitoring API endpoint exposing key operational metrics (active sessions, queue depths, database connection pool utilization, error rates) compatible with standard monitoring platforms (e.g., Prometheus, Datadog).
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational observability enables proactive detection of performance degradation and reduces mean time to resolution for incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support locale-aware formatting of dates, times, numbers, and currency symbols according to each user's configured locale, and shall store all timestamps internally in UTC.
generated_requirements.rationale: Locale-aware formatting prevents user confusion in global deployments and ensures timestamp consistency across time zones.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable session timeout policies (idle and absolute), automatically invalidating sessions that exceed the configured thresholds and requiring re-authentication, with the default idle timeout set to 30 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout enforcement reduces the risk of unauthorized access via unattended authenticated sessions on shared or public devices.

sme_subject: corporate facilities and workplace operations manager
sme_advisory: Conference room and meeting management systems sit at the intersection of facilities operations, IT infrastructure, and HR/compliance workflows. Requirements engineers should pay close attention to real-time concurrency controls (double-booking prevention), integration touchpoints with calendar platforms and directory services, and the operational realities of multi-building, multi-timezone, multilingual deployments. Overlooked areas frequently include equipment lifecycle tracking, no-show/room-release policies, accessibility compliance, and the granular role-based access needs of facilities managers versus end users versus system administrators.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must work with most database management systems (DBMS) on the market whether the DBMS is colocated with the product on the same machine or is located on a different machine on the computer network.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) documents at the end of each business day by consuming the day's POS transaction batch data and applying the active product formula versions to calculate ingredient depletions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core business function: daily IQA generation from sales data is the primary automated workflow of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall resolve all semi-product (sub-recipe) components recursively — performing a full Bill-of-Materials (BOM) explosion — down to raw ingredient quantities before generating any IQA document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Semi-products must be fully decomposed to raw ingredients so that inventory deductions are accurate and traceable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support an ordered ingredient substitution chain (primary → secondary → tertiary) for each formula line, automatically selecting the highest-priority substitute whose current stock level is sufficient to fulfill the required quantity at the time of IQA generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Substitution logic must be deterministic and stock-aware to prevent IQAs from depleting unavailable ingredients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record every substitution decision made during IQA generation, capturing the original ingredient, the substitute selected, the substitution tier used, the stock level at the time of selection, and the formula version active at the time of sale.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full substitution traceability is required for food-cost accuracy, allergen compliance, and audit chain integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall flag any ingredient substitution that alters the declared allergen or nutritional profile of the finished product and place the affected IQA line into a mandatory review state, preventing automatic posting until a designated reviewer approves or overrides the flag.
generated_requirements.rationale: Allergen and nutritional profile changes triggered by substitutions must be reviewed to comply with food labeling regulations such as EU FIC Regulation 1169/2011.

generated_requirements.text: Each IQA document shall follow a double-entry debit/credit model aligned with the restaurant's chart of accounts, recording both the inventory credit (stock reduction) and the corresponding cost-of-goods-sold debit for every ingredient line.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit-compliant food cost accounting requires IQAs to be structured as reversible double-entry transactions consistent with the chart of accounts.

generated_requirements.text: Each IQA document shall maintain an unbroken audit chain linking it to: the originating POS transaction batch ID, the formula version ID active at the time of sale, and the substitution decision log for that document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulators and internal auditors must be able to trace any IQA back to its source sales data and the exact formula and substitution logic applied.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent IQA documents from being backdated into a closed accounting period unless the acting user holds an elevated override permission; such overrides must require the user to supply a mandatory reason code before the document is saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accounting period integrity must be preserved to prevent unauthorized manipulation of closed-period financial records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow formula administrators to define and maintain product formulas, specifying ingredients (including semi-products), quantities, units of measure, and ordered substitution chains for each formula line.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula management is a primary responsibility of the formula administrator role and is the foundation for all IQA calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall version every product formula, recording the creator, creation timestamp, effective start date, and effective end date; historical formula versions must be retained and queryable indefinitely.
generated_requirements.rationale: Menu versioning with effective dates ensures historical sales data remains reconcilable against the formula that was active at the time of each sale.

generated_requirements.text: Formula changes shall not become active until they have been approved through a defined multi-step workflow: the formula administrator submits the change, and a separate approver role explicitly authorizes activation before the effective date.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based workflow gates prevent unauthorized or unreviewed formula changes from affecting live IQA calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to build and organize hierarchical product menus (categories, sub-categories, and items), associating each menu item with exactly one active product formula.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menu management is required to organize the product catalog and link menu items to their formulas for IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduling menu and price changes with a future effective date, ensuring that POS terminals display and apply the correct prices and items only from the scheduled activation date onward.
generated_requirements.rationale: Effective-date scheduling prevents premature activation of price or menu changes and keeps historical sales data reconcilable.

generated_requirements.text: Menu publication to POS terminals shall require explicit authorization from a designated publisher role and must not be triggered by a simple save action.
generated_requirements.rationale: A distinct authorization step for menu publication prevents accidental or unauthorized changes from reaching live POS terminals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate approved menu and price updates to all registered POS terminals atomically; if any terminal fails to acknowledge receipt within a configurable timeout, the system shall automatically roll back the update on all terminals and alert the administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic propagation with rollback ensures all POS terminals remain in a consistent state and prevents partial menu updates that could cause pricing or inventory errors.

generated_requirements.text: POS terminals shall operate in a graceful-degradation mode when connectivity to the central server is lost, continuing to process sales using the last successfully synchronized menu and formula data, and queuing transactions locally for upload upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline capability prevents revenue loss and data gaps during network outages in a Windows-based on-premises environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a unit-of-measure (UOM) conversion table that maps purchase UOMs (e.g., kg, litre, case) to consumption UOMs (e.g., gram, ml, portion) for each ingredient, and shall apply the appropriate conversion factor automatically during BOM explosion and IQA generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ingredients are purchased and consumed in different units; automatic UOM conversion prevents manual calculation errors in stock deductions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Inventory Manager, Formula Administrator, and POS Operator — each granted only the minimum permissions required to perform their designated functions (least-privilege principle).
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege RBAC prevents unauthorized cross-role actions such as POS operators modifying formulas or formula administrators posting finalized IQAs.

generated_requirements.text: POS Operators shall have read-only access to menu data and shall have no write access to product formulas, ingredient master data, inventory records, or IQA documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting POS operators to read-only access on sensitive data prevents accidental or malicious corruption of formulas and inventory records.

generated_requirements.text: Formula Administrators shall be able to create, edit, and submit formulas and menus for approval, but shall not have permission to post finalized IQA documents; IQA posting shall be restricted to the Inventory Manager role or a designated IQA Approver role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Segregation of duties between formula authorship and IQA posting prevents a single user from both defining consumption rules and finalizing their financial impact.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a comprehensive, tamper-evident audit log for all create, update, delete, and status-change operations on formulas, menus, inventory records, and IQA documents, capturing: the user ID, timestamp, action type, affected record ID, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: A complete before/after audit trail is required for regulatory compliance, internal audits, and forensic investigation of data discrepancies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate variance reports comparing theoretical ingredient consumption (derived from formula-driven IQA calculations) against actual inventory depletion recorded in the inventory ledger, and shall automatically flag any line where the variance exceeds a configurable threshold percentage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Variance reporting enables management to detect food cost leakage, waste, theft, or formula inaccuracies before they compound into significant financial losses.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to record manual inventory adjustments (e.g., waste, spoilage, physical count corrections), each requiring a mandatory reason code and generating a corresponding audit log entry and accounting entry consistent with the double-entry model.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual adjustments are a routine inventory management activity that must be controlled and traceable to maintain ledger accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable variance threshold per ingredient category or individual ingredient, allowing administrators to set different tolerance levels (e.g., ±2% for high-cost proteins, ±5% for dry goods) for variance flagging.
generated_requirements.rationale: Different ingredient categories have different cost sensitivities; configurable thresholds allow targeted monitoring without alert fatigue.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition of allergen attributes for each ingredient and shall automatically compute and display the aggregate allergen profile of any formula or semi-product based on its constituent ingredients.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated allergen aggregation is necessary to support compliance with food labeling regulations and to power the substitution allergen-change flag (R005).

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide each user role with a role-specific dashboard and navigation structure that surfaces only the functions and data relevant to that role, hiding inaccessible features rather than displaying them as disabled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-tailored UX reduces cognitive load, minimizes user error, and reinforces access control by not exposing unauthorized functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous access by at least 20 concurrent users across all roles without degradation of response times, using optimistic or pessimistic locking strategies to prevent race conditions on shared records such as inventory stock levels and formula versions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrent POS sales, inventory adjustments, and formula edits can create race conditions that corrupt stock levels or produce incorrect IQA documents if concurrency is not managed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the automated daily IQA generation process — including full BOM explosion, substitution resolution, and document creation — for a sales batch of up to 10,000 transaction lines within 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-of-day IQA generation must complete within a reasonable window to allow timely review and posting before the next business day begins.

generated_requirements.text: All interactive UI operations (form loads, record saves, search queries) shall return a response to the user within 3 seconds under normal operating load on the target hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive UI is critical for POS operators and inventory managers who perform high-frequency, time-sensitive transactions.

generated_requirements.text: POS terminal menu synchronization shall complete propagation to all registered terminals within 60 seconds of an authorized publish action under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely menu synchronization ensures all terminals reflect current prices and items, preventing pricing discrepancies during service.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via username and password at minimum, with support for integration with Windows Active Directory for single sign-on; passwords must be stored using a strong one-way hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2).
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure authentication is the first line of defense against unauthorized access to sensitive financial and inventory data.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between POS terminals and the central server shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit prevents interception of transaction data and credentials on the local network.

generated_requirements.text: The audit log shall be stored in a write-once, append-only structure with integrity verification (e.g., cryptographic hash chaining) to prevent tampering or deletion of historical entries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tamper-evident audit logs are required for regulatory compliance and to ensure the integrity of the audit chain linking IQAs to their source data.

generated_requirements.text: User sessions shall automatically time out after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes), requiring re-authentication before the session is resumed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access from unattended terminals, particularly in shared POS environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run natively on Windows 10 and Windows 11 operating systems and shall not require any cloud-hosted services as a runtime dependency for core functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: The deployment environment is Windows-based on existing hardware; cloud dependencies would violate the constraint of operating without hardware upgrades or cloud-only services.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate fully on the restaurant's existing hardware without requiring CPU, RAM, or storage upgrades, and shall publish minimum hardware specifications that are validated against the existing estate before deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Constraining the system to existing hardware prevents unplanned capital expenditure and ensures the deployment is feasible within the current infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (e.g., 06:00–24:00 local time), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside of peak service periods.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during business hours is critical to prevent revenue loss and operational disruption at POS terminals and in inventory management workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at least once every 24 hours and shall support point-in-time recovery to within 1 hour of any failure event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups and point-in-time recovery protect against data loss from hardware failure, software errors, or accidental deletion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and recover from POS terminal connection failures automatically, re-queuing any pending synchronization messages and retrying delivery without manual intervention, up to a configurable maximum retry count.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic retry and recovery prevents data loss and ensures eventual consistency between the central server and POS terminals after transient network failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with clearly separated layers (presentation, business logic, data access), and each module shall be independently testable with a minimum unit test coverage of 80%.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture and test coverage reduce the cost and risk of future changes to complex business rules such as substitution logic and BOM explosion.

generated_requirements.text: All configurable business parameters — including variance thresholds, session timeout durations, substitution fallback behavior, accounting period boundaries, and POS sync timeout values — shall be manageable through an administrator configuration interface without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing configuration allows business rules to be adjusted quickly in response to operational changes without involving development resources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text and tooltips for all data entry fields, and shall include a searchable user manual accessible from within the application, tailored to each user role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help and role-tailored documentation reduce training time and user error, particularly for complex workflows such as formula definition and IQA review.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to Windows UI design conventions (e.g., standard controls, keyboard navigation, tab order) to minimize the learning curve for staff already familiar with Windows-based applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to familiar UI conventions reduces training overhead and accelerates user adoption in a Windows-based deployment environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, actionable error messages when a business rule violation occurs (e.g., insufficient stock for substitution, closed accounting period, missing reason code), specifying the cause and the corrective action required.
generated_requirements.rationale: Actionable error messages reduce resolution time and prevent users from being blocked by cryptic system errors during time-sensitive operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all application errors and exceptions to a structured log file with severity levels, timestamps, and stack traces, and shall provide an administrator interface for viewing and filtering these logs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Structured error logging accelerates diagnosis and resolution of production issues, reducing system downtime and support costs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce optimistic concurrency control on formula and inventory records, detecting and rejecting conflicting concurrent edits with a user-friendly conflict resolution prompt rather than silently overwriting changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Optimistic concurrency control prevents silent data corruption when multiple users simultaneously edit the same formula or inventory record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to define and manage accounting periods (open, closed, locked states) and shall enforce period-state rules across all IQA posting, manual adjustment, and reversal operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accounting period management is a prerequisite for enforcing the backdating restriction (R008) and maintaining financial record integrity across reporting periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support full reversal of any posted IQA document, generating an equal and opposite accounting entry and linking the reversal document to the original IQA in the audit trail, with a mandatory reason code required for the reversal.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reversibility of IQA documents is required by the double-entry accounting model and enables correction of erroneous postings without destroying the audit trail.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete version history of each menu structure, recording the effective date, the publishing user, and the full snapshot of menu items and prices at each version, so that historical POS sales can be reconciled against the menu version active at the time of the transaction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Menu version history is essential for reconciling historical sales data and for audit purposes when prices or items change over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an ingredient master data management module allowing Inventory Managers to create, update, and deactivate ingredient records, including attributes for name, category, allergen flags, nutritional data, default UOM, purchase UOM, and cost price; all changes shall be audit-logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: A centralized, audited ingredient master is the foundational data source for formula management, allergen compliance, UOM conversion, and cost accounting.

sme_subject: restaurant operations and inventory control specialist
sme_advisory: This platform sits at the intersection of inventory control, recipe/formula management, and POS integration — a combination where small data errors compound quickly into significant food cost variances and compliance gaps. The requirements engineer should pay close attention to how substitution logic interacts with cost accounting and audit trails, since ingredient swaps must be traceable for both food-cost accuracy and regulatory purposes. Special care is needed around concurrency and role segregation, as simultaneous POS sales, inventory adjustments, and formula edits can create race conditions that corrupt stock levels or produce incorrect Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) documents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow new customers to register an account by providing their full name, email address, password, and country of residence, and shall send a verification email before activating the account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account registration is the entry point to the customer journey and email verification reduces fraudulent account creation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to select either a pay-as-you-go plan (individual movie purchases) or an unlimited subscription plan during or after registration, and shall clearly display the pricing, benefits, and differences between the two plans before selection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Plan selection is a core business rule that drives the entire payment and access model for the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to browse the movie catalog by category, genre, release year, language, and country of origin, and shall display each movie's title, synopsis, cast, director, duration, NFVCB age rating, and thumbnail image.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rich catalog browsing enables content discovery and ensures mandatory NFVCB rating information is surfaced to users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full-text search capability over the movie catalog, supporting search by title, actor name, director name, genre, and keyword, and shall return ranked results within 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Search is essential for content discovery across a large catalog and the 2-second threshold maintains a responsive user experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce geo-restriction rules on a per-film basis, blocking or allowing playback and purchase based on the customer's detected IP geolocation in accordance with each film's territory-specific distribution rights agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: African film distributors commonly grant territory-specific licences; failure to enforce geo-restrictions constitutes a licensing breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the applicable NFVCB age rating (e.g. G, PG, 12, 15, 18) prominently on each movie's detail page and shall enforce age-gating for restricted content categories by verifying the customer's declared date of birth before allowing purchase or playback.
generated_requirements.rationale: NFVCB classification obligations require both display and enforcement of age ratings on the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to purchase individual movies (pay-as-you-go) or access all available catalog titles (unlimited plan) by paying via credit/debit card or pre-paid card, and shall confirm the transaction with an on-screen receipt and email confirmation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting both payment methods and both plan types is a core commercial requirement of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall tokenise all payment card data at the point of entry using a PCI DSS-compliant payment gateway and shall never store raw Primary Account Numbers (PANs) on platform servers.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI DSS mandates tokenisation and prohibits raw PAN storage to protect cardholder data from breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage the full pre-paid card lifecycle including: batch generation and issuance to resellers, unique card code assignment, activation, redemption against a customer account, balance/credit tracking, expiry date enforcement, and detection and rejection of duplicate or already-redeemed card codes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-paid cards require end-to-end transactional integrity to prevent fraud, duplicate redemption, and revenue leakage.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful purchase of a movie under the pay-as-you-go plan, the system shall grant the customer a 48-hour (two-day) viewing licence starting from the first play event, and shall display a real-time countdown (e.g. 'Your access expires in 31 hours 14 minutes') on the movie player and account library.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 48-hour viewing window is a core business and licensing rule that must be clearly communicated and enforced.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce the 48-hour viewing window using a DRM licence server (supporting Widevine, FairPlay, and PlayReady) that issues time-bounded playback tokens and revokes them precisely at licence expiry, terminating any active streaming session at that point.
generated_requirements.rationale: DRM enforcement is required to honour distribution rights agreements and prevent licence extension beyond the contractually permitted window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent screen capture, unauthorised download, and side-loading of streamed content by enforcing DRM output protection controls and shall not expose unencrypted media streams at any point in the delivery pipeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Content piracy protection is a contractual obligation to rights holders and a prerequisite for securing distribution licences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support Adaptive Bitrate Streaming (ABR) with a minimum of four quality tiers (240p, 480p, 720p, 1080p) and shall automatically switch quality levels based on the customer's available bandwidth without requiring manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: ABR is critical for users on variable 3G/4G connections prevalent across Nigerian and broader African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall persist a playback bookmark (resume point) for each customer-movie combination and shall automatically resume playback from the last watched position after a session drop, browser refresh, or re-login, without requiring re-purchase or re-authentication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unreliable connectivity in African markets makes graceful session recovery essential to user trust and satisfaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated customers to submit a written review and star rating (1–5) for any movie they have purchased, and shall hold all submitted reviews in a moderation queue before publication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community reviews build platform engagement but must be moderated before publication to prevent harmful content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically screen all submitted reviews using a profanity and hate-speech filter tuned for Nigerian English and Nigerian Pidgin, flagging high-risk submissions for human review escalation, and shall maintain a full audit trail of every moderation decision including the moderator identity, timestamp, and action taken.
generated_requirements.rationale: Content moderation must account for Nigerian English and Pidgin idioms to avoid both over-blocking legitimate content and under-blocking harmful content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide customers with a personal account dashboard where they can view their purchase history, active viewing licences with countdown timers, subscription plan status, saved payment methods (tokenised), and submitted reviews.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account self-service reduces support burden and gives customers transparency over their entitlements and data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to exercise their data subject rights under the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 (NDPA) and GDPR (where applicable) — including the right to access, correct, and delete their personal data — through a self-service interface, and shall fulfil verified requests within 30 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: NDPA 2023 and GDPR mandate that data subjects can exercise access, correction, and erasure rights within defined timeframes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to add, edit, and remove movies from the catalog, including uploading video files, poster images, metadata (title, synopsis, cast, director, genre, language, release year, NFVCB rating), and per-territory geo-restriction rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Catalog management is a core back-office function required to keep content current and correctly licensed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to manage customer accounts, including viewing account details, resetting passwords, suspending or reactivating accounts, and issuing refunds, with every action logged with the administrator's identity, timestamp, and before/after state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrator account management requires a full audit trail for accountability, dispute resolution, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to create, edit, and delete movie categories and genres, and to assign or reassign movies to one or more categories.
generated_requirements.rationale: Category management enables administrators to organise the catalog in culturally relevant ways for the target audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to generate pre-paid card batches, assign them to reseller accounts, set denomination values and expiry dates, activate individual or bulk cards, and view the redemption status of every card in the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full administrative control over the pre-paid card lifecycle is required to manage reseller relationships and prevent fraud.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to review, approve, reject, or escalate customer movie reviews from a moderation queue interface, with the ability to add a moderation note, and shall notify the customer of the outcome of their submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Human moderation escalation is required for edge cases that automated filters cannot reliably resolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a sales and analytics dashboard displaying, at minimum: total revenue by period, revenue split by payment method (credit card vs. pre-paid card), subscription plan distribution and churn rate by plan type, per-region viewership heatmaps, top-performing movies, and peak usage windows displayed in West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1).
generated_requirements.rationale: The Manager requires African-market-specific KPIs to make informed business decisions about content acquisition and marketing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect a potential personal data breach and notify the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) within 72 hours of detection, and shall notify affected data subjects without undue delay, in accordance with NDPA 2023 requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: NDPA 2023 mandates a 72-hour breach notification window to the NDPC, with failure constituting a regulatory offence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) with at minimum three distinct roles — Customer, Administrator, and Manager — ensuring that each role can only access the functions and data explicitly permitted for that role.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC enforces the principle of least privilege and prevents unauthorised access to sensitive administrative and financial functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display all prices primarily in Nigerian Naira (NGN) and shall optionally display equivalent amounts in USD and GBP based on the customer's locale or preference, using exchange rates refreshed at least once every 24 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: NGN as the primary currency reflects the core target market, while optional USD/GBP display supports the global user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall format dates, times, and numbers according to the customer's detected or selected locale, defaulting to Nigerian English (en-NG) conventions, and shall present all platform-generated timestamps to Nigerian users in West Africa Time (WAT, UTC+1).
generated_requirements.rationale: Locale-aware formatting is a core localisation requirement that prevents confusion and builds trust with the primary audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all administrative actions — including catalog edits, account suspensions, pre-paid card activations, content moderation decisions, and role changes — capturing the actor's identity, action type, timestamp, and before/after state, and shall retain these logs for a minimum of 5 years.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit logging is required for regulatory compliance, dispute resolution, and internal accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support lawful cross-border transfer of EU/UK users' personal data by implementing Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or equivalent approved transfer mechanisms where personal data is routed through non-adequate third countries, in compliance with GDPR and UK GDPR.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR and UK GDPR require a lawful transfer mechanism for personal data flows to non-adequate countries, which applies when the platform serves EU/UK users.

generated_requirements.text: The video streaming infrastructure shall sustain a peak concurrent viewer load of at least 10,000 simultaneous streams without degradation in playback quality or buffering ratio exceeding 1%.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalable streaming capacity is essential to handle peak demand events such as new Nollywood releases without service degradation.

generated_requirements.text: All web pages shall achieve a Time to First Byte (TTFB) of under 800 ms and a Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) of under 3 seconds when accessed on a 3G connection (minimum 1 Mbps), to accommodate users on variable mobile networks in Nigeria and across Africa.
generated_requirements.rationale: Page load performance on low-bandwidth connections is critical for usability in African markets where 3G is the dominant access method.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall achieve a monthly uptime of at least 99.9% (excluding scheduled maintenance windows of no more than 4 hours per month, communicated at least 48 hours in advance), measured across all customer-facing services.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential for a streaming platform where downtime directly results in lost revenue and customer dissatisfaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement end-to-end TLS 1.2 or higher encryption for all data in transit between the client browser and platform servers, and shall encrypt all personally identifiable information (PII) and payment-related data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit and at rest is a baseline security control required by PCI DSS, NDPA, and GDPR.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrator and manager accounts, and shall offer MFA as an opt-in feature for customer accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA significantly reduces the risk of account takeover, particularly for privileged administrative accounts with access to sensitive data and functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement rate limiting and CAPTCHA challenges on authentication, registration, and payment endpoints to mitigate brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, and automated card-testing fraud.
generated_requirements.rationale: Payment and authentication endpoints are high-value targets for automated attacks that can result in financial fraud and account compromise.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines, including sufficient colour contrast ratios, keyboard navigability, screen-reader compatibility, and descriptive alt text for all non-decorative images.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance ensures the platform is accessible to users with disabilities and supports the platform's mandate of broad global reach.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's visual design shall employ culturally resonant imagery, colour palettes, typography, and microcopy — including Nigerian English idioms — that reflect African cultural identity, while remaining welcoming and comprehensible to a global audience.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cultural resonance in UX design is a trust-building requirement for the primary Nigerian/African audience and a key differentiator for the platform.

generated_requirements.text: A first-time customer shall be able to complete the full journey from registration through plan selection, payment, and first movie playback in no more than 10 minutes, with no more than 5 distinct screens or steps.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimising onboarding friction is critical for conversion rates, particularly for users on mobile devices with limited data budgets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be horizontally scalable, supporting auto-scaling of web, application, and streaming delivery tiers to handle traffic spikes of up to 5× baseline load within 5 minutes of detection, without manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-scaling is required to handle unpredictable demand spikes associated with major Nollywood releases or promotional events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any single infrastructure component failure (e.g. database node, application server, CDN edge node) within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 15 minutes and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 5 minutes, using automated failover and geo-redundant data replication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tight RTO/RPO targets protect against data loss and prolonged outages that would breach the 99.9% availability SLA.

generated_requirements.text: The platform codebase shall follow a modular, well-documented architecture with a minimum of 80% automated test coverage (unit and integration), and all public APIs shall be versioned to allow backward-compatible evolution.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage and modular architecture reduce the cost and risk of future feature additions, regulatory changes, and bug fixes.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall be deployable on any major cloud provider (AWS, GCP, or Azure) without vendor-specific lock-in at the application layer, using containerised workloads (Docker/Kubernetes) and infrastructure-as-code definitions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud portability protects the business from vendor price increases and enables deployment in African cloud regions as they mature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver video content via a Content Delivery Network (CDN) with Points of Presence (PoPs) in or near West Africa (e.g. Lagos, Johannesburg) to minimise latency and buffering for the primary user base.
generated_requirements.rationale: CDN PoPs close to the user base are essential for low-latency streaming on the variable network conditions prevalent in Nigeria and West Africa.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support internationalisation (i18n) such that all user-facing text is externalised into locale resource files, enabling the addition of new language locales (e.g. Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa, French) without requiring code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalised locale resources allow the platform to expand language support to major Nigerian and African languages without engineering rework.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS mitigation controls at the network perimeter to protect all customer-facing and administrative endpoints from volumetric and application-layer attacks.
generated_requirements.rationale: WAF and DDoS protection are baseline infrastructure security controls for a public-facing e-commerce and streaming platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conduct and document a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) prior to launch and whenever a new high-risk processing activity is introduced, in compliance with NDPA 2023 and GDPR requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: DPIAs are a legal obligation under both NDPA 2023 and GDPR for high-risk personal data processing activities such as payment processing and behavioural analytics.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, plain-language privacy notice and obtain explicit, informed consent from customers before collecting personal data, with separate consent granularity for essential processing, analytics, and marketing communications, in compliance with NDPA 2023 and GDPR.
generated_requirements.rationale: Granular, informed consent is a legal requirement under NDPA 2023 and GDPR and builds user trust in the platform's data handling practices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with exportable reports (CSV and PDF) for all analytics dashboard data, with configurable date ranges and filters, to support offline analysis and regulatory reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable reports enable the Manager to perform deeper analysis and satisfy any regulatory or investor reporting obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, persistent notification to customers when a purchased movie's 48-hour viewing window is within 6 hours of expiry, prompting them to continue watching or re-purchase, across both the account dashboard and the movie player interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive expiry notifications improve customer experience, reduce support queries, and create a re-purchase conversion opportunity.

sme_subject: African digital media licensing and content distribution specialist
sme_advisory: IzognMovies operates at the intersection of African digital media licensing, cross-border content distribution, and e-commerce — a space with unique regulatory, cultural, and technical pressures that generalist engineers frequently underestimate. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to territorial content licensing restrictions, the realities of variable internet infrastructure across African markets (especially Nigeria), and the compliance obligations that arise from handling payment card data and personal data across multiple jurisdictions. Particular care should be taken around the two-day viewing window enforcement, pre-paid card lifecycle management, and culturally resonant UX — these are not cosmetic concerns but core functional and trust-building requirements for the target audience.
