generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a RESTful web service endpoint that accepts inbound lead submissions from authorized external vendors in a structured payload format (e.g., JSON or XML).
generated_requirements.rationale: The system is defined as an automated web service that receives leads from external vendors, requiring a well-defined ingestion interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate each inbound lead payload against a per-vendor configuration profile that defines the accepted field schema, required fields, field data types, and permissible values, rejecting any payload that does not conform to the vendor-specific profile.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendor lead purchase agreements define distinct accepted schemas; a single global schema is insufficient to enforce per-vendor contractual obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain configurable scoring weight parameters at both the enterprise (corporate) level and the individual school/program level, where school/program-level parameters override enterprise-level parameters for the same scoring dimension, and enterprise-level parameters apply by default when no school/program override exists.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case requires configurable corporate and school-defined parameters with clear precedence rules to avoid breaking enterprise-wide logic when school overrides are applied.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compute a composite lead score for each valid inbound lead by applying the applicable scoring weights to contact criteria (e.g., phone reachability, email validity, address completeness) and academic criteria (e.g., program of interest match, enrollment timeline, education level) as defined in the active scoring parameter configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Composite scoring across contact and academic dimensions is the core business rule that drives all downstream disposition decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify each scored lead into one of three disposition buckets — Accepted, Manual Review, or Rejected — based on configurable upper and lower composite score thresholds, where scores at or above the upper threshold are Accepted, scores between the thresholds (inclusive) are placed in Manual Review, and scores below the lower threshold are Rejected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precise, configurable thresholds for the three disposition buckets are the primary business rules driving the entire lead routing workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically forward Accepted leads to the enterprise system via a defined integration interface, including all lead data fields and sufficient metadata (geography, program of interest, enrollment timeline, and representative assignment attributes) to support downstream auto-representative assignment workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-scoring leads must be routed to the enterprise system with complete metadata so that auto-representative assignment can execute without additional data lookups.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically assign each Accepted lead to an admissions representative through the enterprise system's auto-assignment workflow, passing representative selection attributes including the lead's geographic region, program of interest, and the receiving representative's current capacity and availability status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-representative assignment is a downstream dependency that requires the system to supply structured routing metadata to function correctly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall place Manual Review leads into a prioritized queue accessible to Enrollment Coordinators, supporting priority ordering by lead score, queue aging alerts when a lead has remained unreviewed beyond a configurable time threshold, and workload balancing rules to distribute leads across available Enrollment Coordinators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual review queues require priority ordering, aging alerts, and workload balancing to prevent queue stagnation that delays enrollment outreach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reject leads that fail schema validation, fall below the configured rejection score threshold, or are flagged as invalid, and shall return a structured rejection response to the originating vendor that includes a rejection reason code drawn from a defined taxonomy that maps to vendor contract terms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendors require structured rejection reason codes that align with contract terms to enable automated billing reconciliation and credit dispute processing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform duplicate lead detection on every inbound lead using fuzzy matching logic across multiple identity fields including name variants, all submitted phone numbers, alternate email addresses, and mailing address, flagging a lead as a duplicate when the composite match score across these fields exceeds a configurable similarity threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exact-match on a single key is insufficient; fuzzy multi-field matching is required to accurately detect leads submitted under slight variations of the same identity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store duplicate leads in a dedicated duplicate reference store, flagging them with the identifier of the original lead record, and shall return a duplicate-specific rejection reason code to the originating vendor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate leads must be retained for reference and vendor dispute resolution while being excluded from active admissions workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture, store, and forward with each lead the vendor-supplied TCPA consent record, including the opt-in timestamp, consent language version identifier, and the prospective student's IP address at time of consent, and shall reject any lead for which this consent record is absent or malformed.
generated_requirements.rationale: TCPA compliance requires documented prior express written consent to be present and passed through to downstream outreach systems to avoid institutional legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall flag any inbound lead that contains indicators of potential identity fraud — including SSN format mismatches, known fraudulent address patterns, or other configurable fraud indicator rules — and route such leads to a separate fraud review queue rather than the standard admissions workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: FTC Red Flags Rule and institutional data governance policies require identity fraud indicators to be detected and isolated before lead data reaches admissions staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support vendor submission of credit validation records to the enterprise billing system for leads that were rejected, including the structured rejection reason code, original lead identifier, submission timestamp, and vendor identifier, enabling automated billing reconciliation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendor contracts include credit/chargeback provisions that require structured, machine-readable rejection data to be submitted to the billing system for automated reconciliation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail for every lead, recording each intermediate scoring component value, the version identifier of the scoring parameter configuration active at time of evaluation, the final disposition decision, the timestamp of each state transition, and the identity of any human user who acts on a manually reviewed lead.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full auditability of scoring inputs, parameter versions, and human actions is required for compliance, vendor dispute resolution, and retroactive correction workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide administrative users with a secured interface to create, update, activate, and deactivate scoring parameter configurations at both the enterprise and school/program levels, with each change versioned and timestamped and the previous configuration retained for audit purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrators must be able to maintain scoring parameters through a controlled interface while preserving historical versions for audit and retroactive analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide administrative users with access to defect reports that surface leads which were processed but subsequently identified as miscategorized (e.g., an Accepted lead that should have been Rejected), and shall support retroactive correction workflows that allow authorized users to reclassify a lead's disposition and trigger the appropriate downstream actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defect reports and retroactive correction capabilities are required to resolve vendor disputes and correct processing errors discovered after initial disposition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), defining at minimum the roles of Vendor (lead submission only), Enrollment Coordinator (manual review queue access), Administrative User (parameter configuration and defect reports), and System Integrator (integration monitoring), with each role restricted to only the functions and data necessary for its responsibilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC is required to enforce least-privilege access across the diverse user population and to support FERPA data segmentation obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement data segmentation controls that distinguish between prospective student lead records and enrolled/applicant student records, applying FERPA-level access restrictions to any lead data that has been merged into or associated with an active student record in the enterprise system.
generated_requirements.rationale: FERPA protections apply once a prospective student becomes an applicant or enrollee, requiring the system to enforce a clear data access boundary at that transition point.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support jurisdiction-aware data handling for prospective student records, including the ability to tag records with the applicable jurisdiction (e.g., California for CCPA), enforce jurisdiction-specific consent requirements, and process data deletion and portability requests in accordance with applicable state privacy laws.
generated_requirements.rationale: State-level privacy laws such as CCPA impose consent, deletion, and portability obligations that vary by jurisdiction and must be handled programmatically.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that lead routing logic and representative assignment algorithms do not expose, utilize, or enable any compensation metric tied to the enrollment of individual students, and shall log a compliance flag if any routing configuration change is detected that could introduce incentive-based routing.
generated_requirements.rationale: HEA and accreditor standards prohibit incentive compensation tied to individual student enrollment, requiring the system to actively prevent commission-based routing schemes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver a rejection notification to the originating vendor within 72 hours of lead receipt for all Rejected leads, including the structured rejection reason code and the original lead reference identifier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendor SLA provisions in lead purchase agreements typically require rejection notification within 72 hours, making this a contractual deadline the system must meet.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a configurable rejection reason code taxonomy, where each code includes a unique identifier, a human-readable description, a mapping to the relevant vendor contract clause, and a flag indicating whether the code qualifies the lead for vendor credit submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: A structured, contract-mapped rejection reason taxonomy is required to enable automated billing reconciliation and to support vendor dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle failures in the integration with the enterprise system by queuing Accepted leads for retry with exponential back-off, alerting system administrators after a configurable number of consecutive failures, and ensuring no Accepted lead is permanently lost due to a transient integration outage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reliable delivery of Accepted leads to the enterprise system is critical to enrollment yield; transient failures must not result in data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle failures in the integration with the enterprise billing system by queuing credit validation records for retry, logging the failure with full payload details, and alerting administrators, ensuring no credit record is lost due to a transient billing system outage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Billing reconciliation depends on reliable credit record delivery; lost records would create vendor billing disputes and financial liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process each inbound lead — from receipt through final disposition (Accepted forwarding, Manual Review queuing, or Rejected notification) — within a maximum end-to-end latency of 5 seconds under normal operating load, to meet the SLA thresholds required by downstream auto-representative assignment workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict processing time thresholds are explicitly called out as hard constraints because downstream auto-representative assignment workflows are directly dependent on lead routing latency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sustain a throughput of at least [TBD — to be confirmed with stakeholders] lead submissions per minute without degradation of processing latency beyond the 5-second end-to-end threshold, under peak load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peak lead submission volumes from multiple concurrent vendors must be handled without violating the SLA latency threshold.

generated_requirements.text: The duplicate detection fuzzy matching algorithm shall complete its evaluation for a single lead within 500 milliseconds, inclusive of all identity field comparisons, to remain within the overall 5-second end-to-end processing budget.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fuzzy multi-field matching is computationally intensive and must be time-bounded to avoid becoming a bottleneck in the end-to-end processing pipeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of 99.9% or greater (measured monthly), excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated to vendors at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: As a real-time enrollment operations component, unplanned downtime directly impacts lead intake, vendor SLA compliance, and enrollment yield.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between vendors and the system, between the system and the enterprise system, and between the system and the billing system shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prospective student PII and TCPA consent records are legally sensitive and must be protected against interception during transmission.

generated_requirements.text: All prospective student lead data stored by the system shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent approved encryption standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: Stored PII and consent records are subject to FERPA, TCPA, and state privacy law protections that require data-at-rest encryption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all vendor API clients using API key-based or OAuth 2.0 token-based authentication, and shall reject any lead submission request that does not present a valid, active credential associated with a known vendor profile.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unauthenticated lead submissions could introduce fraudulent data into the admissions pipeline and create legal and billing liability.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative interface shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrative user logins.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrative users have access to scoring parameter configuration and sensitive defect reports, making MFA essential to prevent unauthorized configuration changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement rate limiting on the vendor lead submission endpoint, enforcing a configurable maximum number of submissions per vendor per time window, and returning a standardized HTTP 429 response with a Retry-After header when the limit is exceeded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rate limiting protects the system from denial-of-service attacks and prevents a single vendor from monopolizing processing capacity.

generated_requirements.text: The audit trail records produced by the system shall be immutable — no user, including administrative users, shall be able to modify or delete an audit record — and shall be stored in a tamper-evident log store.
generated_requirements.rationale: Immutable audit logs are required for regulatory compliance, vendor dispute resolution, and forensic investigation of miscategorized leads.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all lead records, audit trail entries, TCPA consent records, and rejection notifications for a minimum of 7 years, or longer if required by applicable federal, state, or institutional data retention policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term retention is required to support TCPA litigation defense, FERPA compliance, vendor contract dispute resolution, and institutional audit obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and resume normal lead processing within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 1 hour, with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 15 minutes of lead data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enrollment operations and vendor SLA compliance require rapid recovery from failures with minimal data loss to avoid downstream enrollment and billing impacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular, service-oriented architecture that allows individual components (e.g., scoring engine, duplicate detection, enterprise integration adapter, billing integration adapter) to be updated or replaced independently without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scoring rules, integration contracts, and compliance requirements will evolve over time; modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of change.

generated_requirements.text: All scoring parameter configurations, vendor profiles, rejection reason code taxonomies, and disposition thresholds shall be stored as data-driven configurations (not hard-coded), modifiable through the administrative interface without requiring a software deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business rules and vendor contract terms change frequently; data-driven configuration eliminates deployment overhead for routine parameter updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a comprehensive operational dashboard and structured log output (e.g., JSON-formatted logs) compatible with enterprise SIEM and log aggregation platforms, exposing metrics including lead volume by vendor, disposition distribution, processing latency percentiles, integration error rates, and queue depth.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility is required to detect processing anomalies, SLA breaches, and integration failures before they impact enrollment operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system's integration interfaces with the enterprise system and the billing system shall be defined using versioned API contracts (e.g., OpenAPI specifications), and the system shall support at least one prior API version concurrently to allow downstream systems to migrate without forced simultaneous upgrades.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned, documented integration contracts reduce coupling and allow enterprise and billing systems to evolve independently of the Lead Washing System.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on at least two major cloud infrastructure providers (e.g., AWS and Azure) or on-premises infrastructure, with no hard dependencies on provider-specific proprietary services that would prevent migration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Institutional cloud strategy may change; avoiding vendor lock-in ensures the system can be migrated without a full rebuild.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability by administrative users with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance is a legal and institutional obligation and ensures the administrative interface is usable by all authorized personnel.

generated_requirements.text: The Enrollment Coordinator manual review interface shall display all information required to make a disposition decision — including the full lead record, each scoring component value, the applicable scoring parameter version, and the lead's queue age — on a single screen without requiring navigation to secondary views.
generated_requirements.rationale: Coordinators must make rapid, informed disposition decisions; requiring navigation to multiple screens increases review time and the risk of queue stagnation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a configurable queue aging alert — delivered via email and/or in-application notification — to the assigned Enrollment Coordinator and their supervisor when a Manual Review lead has remained unactioned beyond a configurable aging threshold (default: 24 hours).
generated_requirements.rationale: Queue aging alerts are required to prevent stagnation in the manual review queue that would delay enrollment outreach and potentially violate vendor SLA timelines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and expose, for each processed lead, a complete provenance record that includes: the originating vendor identifier, the submission timestamp, the scoring parameter configuration version used, each individual scoring component and its computed value, the final composite score, the disposition assigned, and any human reviewer identity and action timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full lead provenance is required to support retroactive correction, vendor dispute resolution, compliance audits, and defect report generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate that each inbound lead's TCPA consent timestamp predates the lead submission timestamp, and shall reject leads where the consent timestamp is absent, in the future, or more than a configurable maximum age (e.g., 90 days) prior to submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Stale or future-dated consent records are indicators of fraudulent or non-compliant lead generation practices that create TCPA liability for the institution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a vendor-facing API endpoint that allows vendors to query the current status and disposition of previously submitted leads using the original lead reference identifier, subject to vendor authentication and restricted to the vendor's own leads.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendors need visibility into lead disposition status to manage credit dispute windows and reconcile their own billing records within contractual deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce input sanitization and validation on all inbound API payloads to prevent injection attacks (SQL injection, XML/JSON injection, and script injection), and shall return a standardized error response without exposing internal system details when malformed input is detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Vendor-submitted payloads are an external attack surface; injection vulnerabilities could compromise the integrity of lead data and the underlying data store.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce a monthly compliance summary report, available to authorized administrative users, that includes: total leads received by vendor, disposition distribution (Accepted/Manual Review/Rejected/Duplicate/Fraud), TCPA consent validation failure counts, fraud flag counts, average processing latency, and SLA adherence rate for rejection notifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: A periodic compliance summary supports institutional governance, vendor contract management, and regulatory audit readiness across TCPA, FERPA, and state privacy law obligations.

sme_subject: admissions operations and lead acquisition specialist
sme_advisory: The Lead Washing System sits at the intersection of vendor contract management, admissions compliance, and real-time enrollment operations — making it one of the most legally and operationally sensitive components in a higher education CRM ecosystem. Requirements engineers must pay close attention to TCPA and FERPA compliance obligations that govern how prospective student data is collected, stored, routed, and shared with third parties, as violations can result in significant institutional liability. Special care should be given to the configurability of scoring parameters (which must support school-level overrides without breaking enterprise-wide logic), the auditability of every lead disposition decision, and the strict SLA thresholds that downstream auto-representative assignment workflows depend on — any latency or failure in lead routing can directly impact enrollment yield and vendor billing reconciliation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow players to register an account by providing a unique username, a valid email address, and a password, subject to age verification prior to account creation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account registration is the entry point for all players and must capture identity data needed for lobby, matchmaking, and compliance purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present an age-gate step during registration that requires the user to confirm their date of birth; users identified as under 13 (US) or under 16 (EU) shall be blocked from completing registration unless verifiable parental consent is provided.
generated_requirements.rationale: COPPA and GDPR-K mandate verifiable parental consent before collecting personal data from minors, and an age-gate is the required enforcement mechanism.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a cookie and local-storage consent notice to users on first visit, clearly describing what data is stored and for what purpose, and shall not set non-essential cookies or local storage entries until the user grants explicit consent.
generated_requirements.rationale: PECR and ePrivacy regulations require informed consent before using cookies or local storage for session management in applicable jurisdictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide authenticated players with self-service mechanisms to access, rectify, and permanently erase their personal data (right to be forgotten) in compliance with GDPR Article 17, completing erasure requests within 30 days.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR Articles 15–17 grant data subjects the rights of access, rectification, and erasure, which must be operationally supported by the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all player personally identifiable information (PII) — including email addresses, usernames, and IP addresses — on servers located within the EU or on servers covered by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or equivalent GDPR Chapter V safeguards when stored outside the EU.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR Chapter V prohibits cross-border transfers of personal data to third countries without adequate safeguards such as SCCs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a shared lobby in which all authenticated, currently online players are listed with their username and availability status, refreshed in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: A shared lobby is the core matchmaking surface that allows players to discover and select available opponents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce lobby presence heartbeats: each connected client must send a heartbeat signal to the server at least once every 15 seconds, and the server shall automatically remove any player entry from the lobby whose heartbeat has not been received within 30 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Heartbeat-based presence detection prevents stale lobby entries from accumulating and ensures the lobby accurately reflects currently available players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent duplicate session registrations: if a player who is already registered in the lobby or in an active game attempts to open a second session, the system shall invalidate the older session and notify the affected client.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate sessions would corrupt lobby state and game integrity, so only one active session per player must be permitted at any time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement atomic opponent slot reservation so that when a player selects an opponent from the lobby, the opponent's slot is locked server-side in a single atomic operation, preventing two players from simultaneously selecting the same opponent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Without atomic reservation, a race condition could cause two players to both believe they have claimed the same opponent, corrupting matchmaking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow each player, at the start of a game, to place their fleet of ships on a personal 10×10 defensive grid according to standard Battleship rules: ships must be placed horizontally or vertically, must not overlap, and must not extend beyond grid boundaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ship placement is the foundational setup phase of Battleship and its rules must be precisely specified to ensure a valid game state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all ship placement submissions server-side, rejecting any placement that violates grid boundaries, causes ship overlap, or does not conform to the required fleet composition, and shall return a descriptive error to the client without advancing game state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Server-side validation is mandatory to prevent cheating; client-side validation alone cannot be trusted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a standard fleet composition per player consisting of: one Carrier (5 cells), one Battleship (4 cells), one Cruiser (3 cells), one Submarine (3 cells), and one Destroyer (2 cells).
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined fleet composition is required to ensure game balance and a deterministic win condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage turn progression automatically: after a player submits a valid shot, the server shall update the game state, notify both players of the outcome, and transfer the turn to the opposing player.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated turn management ensures strict alternation and prevents a player from taking multiple consecutive shots.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all shot coordinate submissions server-side, rejecting shots that target out-of-bounds coordinates, coordinates already targeted in the current game, or shots submitted out of turn, and shall return a descriptive error without advancing game state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Server-side shot validation prevents cheating and ensures game state integrity regardless of client behaviour.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all game action endpoints (shot submission, forfeit) as idempotent operations, identified by a unique client-generated action token, so that duplicate requests caused by network retries produce the same outcome without duplicating game state changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Idempotency prevents duplicate game actions from corrupting state when clients retry requests after network timeouts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use WebSockets to push real-time game events — including hit notifications, miss notifications, ship-sinking notifications, and turn-change notifications — to both players immediately after each validated game action, without requiring client polling.
generated_requirements.rationale: A push-based event architecture minimises latency and server load compared to polling, which is critical for a real-time multiplayer experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the complete game state server-side at all times so that a player who disconnects mid-game can rejoin the same game session within 5 minutes, restoring the full board state, turn order, and shot history without data loss or turn corruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session persistence and reconnection handling are essential to prevent unfair game termination due to transient network failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the active player when their opponent disconnects, display a countdown timer of 5 minutes, and automatically award a win to the active player if the disconnected player does not reconnect within that window.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined disconnection timeout policy ensures games are not left indefinitely suspended while still giving disconnected players a fair opportunity to return.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a player to forfeit an active game at any time by selecting a forfeit action; upon confirmation, the server shall immediately record the forfeit, declare the opponent the winner, and return both players to the lobby.
generated_requirements.rationale: A forfeit mechanism gives players a graceful exit and ensures game state is cleanly resolved rather than left in an indeterminate condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the win condition after each shot: if all cells of all ships in a player's fleet have been hit, the system shall immediately declare the opposing player the winner, record the game outcome, and transition both players back to the lobby.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated win detection ensures the game concludes correctly and promptly when the victory condition is met.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render each player's defensive grid and offensive tracking grid through a periscope-style visual frame, using nautical-themed imagery consistent across all supported locales.
generated_requirements.rationale: The periscope-style UI is a core product differentiator and must be explicitly specified to guide design and implementation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render each ship on the player's defensive grid as a realistic ship image corresponding to the ship type (Carrier, Battleship, Cruiser, Submarine, Destroyer), scaled to fit the occupied grid cells.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realistic ship imagery is a specified UI requirement that contributes to the nautical theme and player immersion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall play distinct sound effects for the following gameplay events: shot resulting in a hit, shot resulting in a miss, ship sinking, player's own turn starting, opponent's turn starting, game won, and game lost.
generated_requirements.rationale: Event-driven sound effects are explicitly required by the use case and enhance player feedback and immersion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall remain fully playable — including all ship placement, shot submission, turn management, and win detection functionality — if sound effect files or nautical visual theme assets fail to load, displaying functional fallback indicators in place of missing assets.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation ensures asset delivery failures do not block core gameplay, protecting user experience under adverse network or CDN conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall indicate hit and miss outcomes on the offensive tracking grid using both a distinct visual symbol (e.g., an explosion icon for hits and a splash icon for misses) and a colour difference, ensuring the distinction is perceivable without relying on colour alone, in compliance with WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 1.4.1.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 Level AA requires that information conveyed by colour is also available through another visual means to support colour-blind users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the English (en), Spanish (es), and French (fr) locales; all UI strings, button labels, error messages, sound effect labels, date/time formats, and in-game notifications shall be externalised into per-locale resource bundles and rendered in the player's selected language.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalising strings into locale resource bundles is the standard i18n/l10n pattern required to support multiple languages without code changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a player to select their preferred language (English, Spanish, or French) during registration and from within the application settings at any time; the selected locale shall be persisted to the player's profile and applied on every subsequent login.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persisting locale preference ensures a consistent localised experience across sessions without requiring the player to re-select their language each time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all game actions — including ship placements, shot coordinates and outcomes, forfeit events, disconnection events, reconnection events, and game outcomes — with UTC timestamps and the player identifiers of both participants, storing logs for a minimum of 90 days for dispute resolution and analytics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit logging with timestamps and player identifiers is required for dispute resolution, anti-cheat analysis, and operational analytics.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a post-game summary screen displaying the final board states of both players, the sequence of shots taken, the game duration, and the outcome (win/loss/forfeit), accessible to both players immediately after a game concludes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A post-game summary enhances player experience and supports transparency by allowing players to review the game's progression.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a player to mute or unmute all sound effects at any time during gameplay via a clearly labelled audio control, with the mute preference persisted for the duration of the session.
generated_requirements.rationale: An accessible audio control is a basic usability requirement that accommodates players in environments where sound is disruptive.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a monthly uptime of no less than 99.9% (allowing a maximum of approximately 43.8 minutes of unplanned downtime per month), measured at the application load balancer, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: A formally specified uptime SLA is required to operationalise the 'continuously available' product promise and provide a measurable contractual target.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall schedule planned maintenance windows outside of peak usage hours, announce them to registered players at least 24 hours in advance via in-app notification and email, and limit each maintenance window to a maximum of 2 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defining maintenance window policies is necessary to qualify the 99.9% uptime SLA and manage player expectations during planned downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver game event notifications (hits, misses, sinkings, turn changes) to both players via WebSocket within 500 milliseconds of the server validating the triggering action, under normal network conditions (RTT ≤ 150 ms).
generated_requirements.rationale: A sub-500 ms event delivery target ensures the game feels real-time and responsive, which is critical for player engagement in a turn-based multiplayer context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and respond to shot submission API requests within 300 milliseconds at the 95th percentile under a load of up to 10,000 concurrent active game sessions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined server-side response time target under load ensures the system remains responsive as concurrent usage scales.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 simultaneous active game sessions (20,000 concurrent connected players) without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit concurrency targets are required to guide infrastructure sizing and validate that the system meets scalability expectations for a broad international audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally by adding server instances automatically when average CPU utilisation across the application tier exceeds 70% for more than 60 seconds, and shall scale in when utilisation drops below 30% for more than 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-scaling policies ensure the system can handle traffic spikes without manual intervention while controlling infrastructure costs during low-traffic periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the initial page load — including all critical UI assets required to display the lobby — within 3 seconds on a standard broadband connection (≥ 10 Mbps) as measured by the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) metric.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fast initial load time is essential for first-time user retention and is a recognised web performance standard.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be operable by a first-time user with no prior training or tutorial, achieving successful completion of a full game (registration, lobby entry, ship placement, and game play) within 10 minutes of first access, as validated by usability testing with representative users.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires the system to be intuitive enough for new users without prior training, necessitating a measurable usability target.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines, including sufficient colour contrast ratios, keyboard navigability of all interactive elements, and screen-reader-compatible ARIA labels on all game controls and grid cells.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance is required to serve a broad international audience that may include users with visual or motor disabilities, and is particularly important given the colour-heavy nautical theme.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline guidance — such as highlighted valid placement zones during ship placement and a tooltip explaining whose turn it is — without requiring the player to consult external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual in-UI guidance is necessary to meet the no-prior-training usability requirement for first-time players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all API requests using short-lived signed tokens (e.g., JWT with a maximum 1-hour expiry), and shall enforce HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher) for all client-server communications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Token-based authentication over TLS prevents session hijacking and eavesdropping on player data and game state in transit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store player passwords using a strong adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12 or Argon2id) and shall never store or log plaintext passwords.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adaptive password hashing protects player credentials in the event of a database breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall rate-limit login attempts to a maximum of 10 failed attempts per account within any 15-minute window, after which the account shall be temporarily locked for 30 minutes and the registered email notified.
generated_requirements.rationale: Login rate limiting mitigates brute-force credential attacks against player accounts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from a single application server instance failure within 30 seconds, rerouting active WebSocket connections to healthy instances and restoring in-progress game sessions from persistent server-side state without requiring player intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic failover with fast recovery time is required to meet the 99.9% availability SLA and preserve in-progress games during infrastructure failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall replicate game state and player session data to at least one geographically separate standby data store in real time (RPO ≤ 5 seconds), enabling recovery from a primary data store failure with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of no more than 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RPO and RTO targets for data replication ensure game state is not lost and the system recovers quickly from data-layer failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall separate all locale-specific content (UI strings, error messages, date/time formats, sound effect labels) from application logic using an internationalisation (i18n) framework, such that adding a new supported language requires only the addition of a new resource bundle without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict i18n/l10n separation reduces the cost and risk of adding future language support and prevents locale-specific bugs from affecting core logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any major cloud provider (AWS, Azure, or GCP) using containerised application components (e.g., Docker) orchestrated by a container management platform (e.g., Kubernetes), with no hard dependency on a single cloud provider's proprietary services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud-provider portability prevents vendor lock-in and supports data residency compliance by enabling deployment in specific geographic regions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all game and lobby functionality through versioned RESTful APIs and a versioned WebSocket protocol, such that client and server versions can be updated independently without breaking active sessions, and deprecated API versions are supported for a minimum of 6 months after a new version is released.
generated_requirements.rationale: API versioning enables independent evolution of client and server components and protects active users from breaking changes during updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated integration and end-to-end test suites covering all critical game state transitions (ship placement validation, shot validation, turn progression, win detection, forfeit, and disconnection handling), with a minimum code coverage of 80% on server-side game logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated test coverage of critical game logic reduces regression risk and supports confident, frequent deployments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a real-time operational dashboard displaying active session count, lobby player count, WebSocket connection health, server error rates, and p95 API response times, with alerting triggered when any metric breaches defined thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational observability is essential for detecting and responding to incidents quickly enough to maintain the 99.9% availability SLA.

sme_advisory: For a real-time multiplayer Battleship game targeting an international audience, the requirements engineer must pay close attention to the interplay between real-time synchronisation, authoritative server-side game state, and the user experience under adverse network conditions. Turn-based naval combat introduces specific state-machine constraints — such as enforcing strict turn order, validating ship placement rules server-side, and handling mid-game disconnections gracefully — that are easy to underspecify. Given the broad audience (multilingual, potentially minors), compliance obligations around data privacy, age gating, and content localisation must be explicitly addressed alongside the technical quality attributes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at least two distinct user roles: Caller and Supervisor, each with explicitly defined and enforced permission boundaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is foundational to ensuring callers cannot modify schedules and supervisors retain administrative authority.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall be able to create, read, update, and delete caller schedule entries, including shift start time, end time, date, and assigned caller.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full CRUD capability for schedule management is the core functional responsibility of the supervisor role.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall be able to view the complete schedules of all callers in a consolidated calendar or list view, filterable by date range, caller, and shift status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supervisors require a holistic view of all caller schedules to manage staffing coverage and identify gaps.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall be able to advertise open time slots to eligible callers, specifying the date, time range, required skills or qualifications, and a claim deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Open slot advertisement is an explicitly stated feature requirement and supports dynamic staffing adjustments.

generated_requirements.text: Callers shall be able to view their own schedule, including upcoming shifts, past shifts, and any open slots for which they are eligible, but shall have no access to any other caller's schedule data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Caller self-service access must be strictly scoped to prevent lateral exposure of peer HR-sensitive PII.

generated_requirements.text: Callers shall be able to claim an advertised open time slot through the system, subject to supervisor approval or configurable auto-approval rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: A claim workflow closes the loop on open slot advertisement and reduces manual coordination overhead for supervisors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect and prevent scheduling conflicts, including double-booking a caller for overlapping shifts, and shall display a descriptive error message when a conflict is detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict detection prevents scheduling errors that would directly impact staffing levels and service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable maximum weekly hour thresholds per caller and shall warn or block supervisors when a proposed schedule would cause a caller to exceed the defined threshold, supporting FLSA and student employment policy compliance.
generated_requirements.rationale: FLSA compliance and CBA/student employment policy rules require automated hour-limit enforcement to prevent unintentional overtime violations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail of all schedule modifications, recording the actor's user ID, the previous and new values of changed fields, and a UTC timestamp for each change.
generated_requirements.rationale: An audit trail is required for institutional data governance, HR record-keeping accountability, and dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall be able to query and export the audit trail, filterable by date range, caller, and action type (create, update, delete).
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable audit logs support compliance reporting and internal investigations without requiring direct database access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated notifications to callers when their schedule is created, modified, or deleted, via at least one configurable channel (email, SMS, or in-app notification).
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications ensure callers are aware of schedule changes and reduce no-show incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated notifications to eligible callers when a new open time slot is advertised, via the caller's configured notification channel.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive open-slot notifications accelerate slot fill rates and reduce supervisor manual outreach effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated shift reminder notifications to callers a configurable number of hours before each scheduled shift (default: 24 hours).
generated_requirements.rationale: Shift reminders reduce absenteeism and support operational continuity at the TeleServices Center.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall have access to a real-time dashboard displaying current and upcoming coverage levels, headcount by time block, and a count of unfilled open slots.
generated_requirements.rationale: A coverage dashboard gives supervisors immediate situational awareness needed to make staffing decisions during operational hours.

generated_requirements.text: Supervisors shall be able to generate and export schedule reports (PDF and CSV) covering configurable date ranges, individual callers, or the full caller population.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable reports support payroll processing, HR record-keeping, and compliance documentation requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a secure login mechanism integrated with the institution's identity provider (e.g., SAML 2.0 or OAuth 2.0 / OIDC SSO), and shall not store plaintext passwords.
generated_requirements.rationale: Institutional SSO integration centralizes identity management and enforces consistent credential security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated session after a configurable period of inactivity (default: 15 minutes) and require re-authentication before granting further access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout mitigates unauthorized access risk on shared or semi-public call center workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain schedule records and associated audit logs for a minimum of seven (7) years in accordance with institutional HR record-keeping requirements, after which records may be purged per a documented data retention policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data retention compliance is mandated by institutional HR policy and applicable jurisdictional requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable notification preference panel allowing each user to select their preferred notification channel(s) (email, SMS, in-app) and opt out of non-mandatory notification types.
generated_requirements.rationale: User-controlled notification preferences improve adoption and reduce notification fatigue without sacrificing critical alerts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a supervisor-facing alert when a caller's scheduled hours for the current week approach within a configurable threshold (default: 2 hours) of the caller's maximum weekly hour limit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive hour-limit alerts give supervisors time to adjust schedules before an FLSA or CBA violation occurs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum uptime of 99.5% during the TeleServices Center's defined operational hours (to be specified in the SLA), measured on a monthly basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during operational hours is critical because scheduling downtime directly disrupts staffing coordination and service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: Planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled exclusively outside the TeleServices Center's operational hours, and users shall be notified at least 48 hours in advance via email.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting maintenance to off-hours prevents disruption to staffing workflows during active call center operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load any schedule view or dashboard page within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 100 concurrent users) as measured from the time of user request to full page render.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive page loads are essential for usability in a fast-paced call center environment where supervisors and callers need quick access to scheduling information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 200 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the thresholds defined in R023.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to at least 200 concurrent users accommodates peak usage periods such as shift changes and open-slot claim rushes.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, including full keyboard navigability, screen reader compatibility (ARIA landmarks and labels), and a minimum color contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance is mandated for web-based tools at academic institutions and ensures the system is usable by staff with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: A new user (caller or supervisor) with no prior training shall be able to complete their primary role-specific tasks (viewing own schedule for callers; creating and publishing a shift for supervisors) within 10 minutes of first login, as validated by usability testing with representative users.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system is explicitly required to be learnable with minimal training, and a measurable learnability criterion makes this testable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text and tooltips for all non-obvious form fields and actions, without requiring users to navigate away from their current task.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance reduces the learning curve for users with varying technical proficiency and minimizes support requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, human-readable error messages for all validation failures (e.g., scheduling conflicts, hour-limit violations) that describe the problem and suggest a corrective action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Actionable error messages reduce user frustration and enable self-correction without supervisor or IT intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall function correctly and consistently on the latest two major versions of institutionally approved browsers (Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, and Safari) without requiring browser plugins, extensions, or elevated user permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser compatibility with the institution's standardized IT environment ensures the system is deployable without additional endpoint configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully operable on standard desktop and laptop screen resolutions (minimum 1024×768) and shall provide a responsive layout that remains usable on tablet devices (minimum 768px viewport width).
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting a range of screen sizes ensures usability across the variety of devices found in a call center and administrative environment.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the client browser and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher; unencrypted HTTP connections shall be automatically redirected to HTTPS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects HR-sensitive PII and schedule data from interception on shared or institutional networks.

generated_requirements.text: All schedule data and PII stored by the system shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent approved algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects sensitive employee and student data in accordance with institutional data governance and FERPA requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) at the API and data layer, ensuring that no caller can access, modify, or infer another caller's schedule data regardless of client-side manipulation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Server-side RBAC enforcement prevents privilege escalation and lateral data access that client-side controls alone cannot prevent.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication events (successful login, failed login, logout, session timeout) including user ID, IP address, and timestamp, and shall retain these logs for a minimum of one year.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication event logs support security incident investigation and compliance auditing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account after five (5) consecutive failed authentication attempts and require an administrator-initiated or self-service unlock via the institutional identity provider.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks on shared workstation environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall be organized into modular, loosely coupled components with documented APIs, enabling individual modules (e.g., notification engine, scheduling engine) to be updated or replaced without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements and bug fixes as institutional requirements evolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include automated unit and integration test suites with a minimum of 80% code coverage, and all tests shall be executable in a CI/CD pipeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated testing reduces regression risk and accelerates safe deployment of updates to a system where downtime has direct operational impact.

generated_requirements.text: All configurable system parameters (e.g., session timeout duration, maximum weekly hours defaults, notification lead times, operational hours window) shall be manageable through an administrative configuration interface without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing configuration reduces the operational burden on IT staff and allows policy changes to be applied rapidly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a documented, versioned REST API for all core scheduling operations to support potential future integration with HR, payroll, or telephony systems used at the TeleServices Center.
generated_requirements.rationale: A versioned API enables future system integrations without requiring architectural rework, protecting the institution's investment.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall support horizontal scaling of application server instances to handle traffic spikes without manual intervention, using a load balancer and stateless session management.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability ensures the system can accommodate growth in caller headcount or usage intensity without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all schedule data and audit logs, store backups in a geographically separate location, and support point-in-time recovery to within one hour of any data loss event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Robust backup and recovery capabilities protect against data loss that would compromise scheduling continuity and HR record-keeping compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and alert operations staff within 5 minutes of any service degradation or outage affecting availability during the TeleServices Center's operational hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid incident detection minimizes the duration of outages that directly impact staffing coordination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle all schedule data in compliance with FERPA where callers are students, treating schedule records as education-related PII and restricting disclosure to authorized personnel only.
generated_requirements.rationale: FERPA compliance is a legal obligation for university-affiliated systems that store or process student employee data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide supervisors with the ability to define and manage caller eligibility criteria for open slot advertisement (e.g., minimum hours worked, role type, availability flags), ensuring only eligible callers receive and can claim a given open slot.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eligibility filtering ensures open slots are filled by appropriately qualified callers and supports CBA or policy-based assignment rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and display the full change history for each individual schedule entry, accessible to supervisors, showing a chronological list of all modifications with actor, timestamp, and field-level diff.
generated_requirements.rationale: Per-entry change history enables supervisors to quickly investigate scheduling discrepancies and supports accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a supervisor-accessible interface to configure and manage the list of callers, including adding new callers, deactivating former callers, and assigning or modifying role assignments.
generated_requirements.rationale: User lifecycle management within the system ensures access rights remain current as staff turnover occurs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a deactivated caller's account from being used to log in or claim open slots, while preserving all historical schedule and audit data associated with that account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deactivation without deletion preserves HR record integrity while immediately revoking operational access for departed staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall use a consistent visual design language (color palette, typography, iconography, and layout grid) aligned with DePaul University's branding guidelines, contributing to a visually appealing and professional appearance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Institutional branding alignment reinforces trust and familiarity for users who interact with other DePaul digital tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support localization infrastructure (i18n) such that all user-facing strings are externalized and the application can be adapted to additional languages without code changes, even if only English is required at initial launch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Building in localization infrastructure from the outset avoids costly retrofitting if the institution's workforce diversity requires multilingual support in the future.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo a third-party security vulnerability assessment (including OWASP Top 10 testing) prior to initial production deployment and after any major release, with all critical and high findings remediated before go-live.
generated_requirements.rationale: Independent security assessment ensures the system protecting HR-sensitive PII meets institutional and regulatory security standards before exposure to users.

sme_advisory: The DePaul TeleServices Center scheduling tool sits at the intersection of workforce management and operational continuity — any downtime or scheduling error directly impacts staffing levels and service delivery. The requirements engineer should pay close attention to role-based access control boundaries (callers vs. supervisors), real-time schedule visibility, and the operational window during which the system must be available, as these are the most common sources of failure in similar deployments. Special care should be taken to capture non-functional requirements around usability and accessibility, since the user base likely spans varying levels of technical proficiency and may include users with disabilities subject to institutional accessibility mandates.
