generated_requirements.text: The system shall ingest messages from the Customer Service Request (CSR) system and route them asynchronously to the Enterprise Asset Management (EAM/Datastream) system without requiring a direct interface between the two systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core integration pattern of the platform is decoupled, asynchronous message brokering between CSR and EAM.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a globally unique, immutable correlation ID to every message at the point of ingestion, and shall propagate that ID across all processing hops — including CSR origination, middleware routing, EAM delivery, and delivery acknowledgment — to enable end-to-end traceability.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-to-end correlation IDs are required to trace a single service request across all system boundaries for audit, debugging, and FOIA compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce idempotent message processing such that redelivery or retry of a message with the same correlation ID does not result in duplicate transactions being applied to the EAM system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Idempotency prevents duplicate work-order creation or asset record corruption in EAM when retries occur across the CSR-to-EAM pipeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Dead-Letter Queue (DLQ) to which messages are moved after exhausting a configurable number of retry attempts, and shall immediately alert the designated System Administrator via email and dashboard notification upon any message entering the DLQ.
generated_requirements.rationale: DLQ handling with administrator alerting ensures undeliverable messages are surfaced promptly and do not silently fail, protecting municipal service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow System Administrators to configure per-queue retry policies, including maximum retry count, retry interval, and back-off strategy (e.g., fixed, exponential), without requiring a system restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable retry policies allow operational tuning to match the SLA and fault-tolerance requirements of different message types.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable Time-To-Live (TTL) value on each message; upon TTL expiry, the message shall be moved to the DLQ and flagged with an expiry reason code rather than silently discarded.
generated_requirements.rationale: TTL expiry handling prevents stale work-order messages from being delivered to EAM and corrupting asset records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support message transformation between the data formats used by the CSR system and the EAM/Datastream system, conforming to the contractually defined integration specifications (XML/SOAP or REST/JSON) of each connected system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transformation is required to bridge the differing data exchange standards of CSR and EAM without modifying either system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the schema and content of every inbound message against a defined message contract before routing; messages that fail validation shall be rejected, logged with a structured error record, and moved to the DLQ.
generated_requirements.rationale: Schema validation at ingestion prevents malformed messages from propagating downstream and corrupting EAM asset data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall guarantee ordered, at-least-once delivery of messages within a logical message sequence (e.g., all messages belonging to a single service request), and shall provide configurable exactly-once delivery semantics for queues where duplicate suppression is enabled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sequencing guarantees are required to ensure that dependent work-order operations reach EAM in the correct order.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based administrative dashboard that displays real-time Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) including: current throughput (messages per second), queue depth per queue, end-to-end message latency, error rate, and DLQ depth.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time KPI dashboards enable System Administrators and DBAs to monitor platform health and detect degradation before SLAs are breached.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate threshold-based alerts — delivered via dashboard notification, email, and configurable webhook — when any monitored KPI (throughput, queue depth, error rate, latency) exceeds or falls below administrator-defined thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive alerting allows administrators to respond to emerging issues before they impact municipal service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a transaction error management interface through which System Administrators and DBAs can view, filter, search, reprocess, or permanently discard messages in the DLQ, with all such actions recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational triage of failed messages requires a dedicated interface with full auditability of remediation actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support both scheduled and ad-hoc reporting on message traffic, error rates, throughput trends, and latency distributions, with report export in CSV and PDF formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scheduled and ad-hoc reports in standard formats satisfy management reporting, budget-justification, and audit requirements common in municipal IT.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all message payloads, metadata (including correlation ID, timestamps, source, destination, status, and transformation records), and audit log entries for a minimum of seven (7) years in accordance with the Illinois Local Records Act (5 ILCS 160) and City of Chicago Municipal Code Chapter 2-8 retention schedules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Government records-retention law mandates multi-year retention of electronic transaction records, with seven years covering the most stringent applicable classification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support targeted, auditable extraction of message logs and audit trail records in response to Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA, 5 ILCS 140) requests, enabling retrieval of specific records by date range, correlation ID, or originating system without exposing unrelated records.
generated_requirements.rationale: FOIA obligations require the ability to produce specific records on demand while preserving the confidentiality of unrelated municipal data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with least-privilege enforcement, defining at minimum the following roles: System Administrator (full operational control), Database Administrator (data and schema access), Read-Only Auditor (view-only access to logs and reports), and Integration Service Account (programmatic message submission and retrieval only).
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC aligned to City IT security policy and NIST SP 800-53 controls limits the blast radius of compromised credentials and enforces separation of duties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all human users via the City of Chicago's enterprise identity provider using multi-factor authentication (MFA), and shall authenticate integration service accounts using mutual TLS (mTLS) or API key credentials with configurable expiry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication for both human and machine identities is required by City Information Security Policy and NIST SP 800-53 IA controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident, append-only audit log recording all security-relevant events, including: user logins and logouts, role assignments, configuration changes, message reprocessing actions, DLQ operations, and report exports.
generated_requirements.rationale: A tamper-evident audit log is required for FOIA compliance, legal defensibility, and detection of unauthorized administrative actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and flag any message payload containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) as defined under the Illinois Personal Information Protection Act (PIIPA, 815 ILCS 530), and shall enforce additional access controls and logging for retrieval of such messages to support breach-notification obligations.
generated_requirements.rationale: PIIPA breach-notification obligations require the platform to identify and specially protect PII transiting the middleware, such as citizen contact data in CSR payloads.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support versioning of all middleware configurations (routing rules, transformation maps, retry policies, TTL settings, queue definitions) and shall provide a rollback mechanism to restore any prior configuration version, with all changes and rollbacks recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: DoIT change-management and procurement rules require documented configuration versioning and rollback capability for all middleware changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the addition of new processing nodes to the cluster without service interruption, without manual reconfiguration of connected systems (CSR or EAM), and without loss or duplication of in-flight messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent horizontal scaling is required to accommodate growth and changing workloads without impacting municipal service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process at least 95% of messages end-to-end (from ingestion to delivery acknowledgment) within 5 seconds under normal operating load, and shall process 99% of messages within 30 seconds under peak load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict latency thresholds are required to meet SLA obligations for real-time municipal service request processing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sustain a minimum throughput of 500 messages per second under normal operating conditions and shall maintain functional correctness at peak throughput of at least 1,500 messages per second without message loss or ordering violations.
generated_requirements.rationale: High transaction volumes typical of a large municipal service platform require defined throughput floors to ensure the system does not become a bottleneck.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally by adding processing nodes such that throughput capacity increases linearly (within 20% of ideal linear scaling) with each additional node, up to at least 10 nodes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-linear horizontal scalability ensures the platform can accommodate future growth in message volume without architectural redesign.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (no more than approximately 8.7 hours of unplanned downtime per year) during defined business hours (Monday–Friday, 06:00–22:00 Central Time), measured on a rolling 12-month basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-continuous availability during business operations is required to ensure uninterrupted municipal service request processing.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from any single node failure within 60 seconds without loss of committed messages, using redundant node failover and persistent message storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automated failover with no message loss is essential for a platform whose failures have direct public-service consequences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall guarantee zero message loss for any message that has been acknowledged as received by the platform, including during node failures, network partitions, or planned maintenance events.
generated_requirements.rationale: Guaranteed delivery is a foundational reliability requirement for a municipal integration platform where lost messages translate to missed service requests.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all message data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher for all connections between clients, brokers, and downstream systems, and shall reject connections that do not meet the minimum TLS version requirement.
generated_requirements.rationale: TLS 1.2+ encryption in transit is mandated by City of Chicago Information Security Policy and NIST SP 800-53 SC-8 controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all message payloads and metadata at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent NIST-approved algorithm, including data stored in message queues, DLQs, audit logs, and long-term retention storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest for municipal operational data is mandated by City Information Security Policy and NIST SP 800-53 SC-28 controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce message integrity checking using cryptographic hashing (e.g., SHA-256) on all message payloads, detecting and rejecting any message whose hash does not match the value recorded at ingestion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrity checking prevents tampered or corrupted messages from being delivered to EAM, protecting asset record accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authorization checks on every API call and administrative action, ensuring that no user or service account can perform operations outside the permissions defined by their assigned RBAC role, and shall return a structured, non-verbose error for any unauthorized access attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege authorization enforcement is required by NIST SP 800-53 AC controls and City IT security policy to prevent privilege escalation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock any user account after five (5) consecutive failed authentication attempts and shall require administrator intervention or a time-based unlock to restore access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies are required by NIST SP 800-53 AC-7 to mitigate brute-force credential attacks.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative web dashboard shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act, ensuring all dashboard features are operable by users relying on assistive technologies.
generated_requirements.rationale: ADA and Section 508 accessibility standards apply to all web-based administrative interfaces delivered as part of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The administrative dashboard shall load its primary KPI view within 3 seconds on a standard City-issued workstation connected to the City's internal network, under normal platform load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive dashboard load times ensure administrators can quickly assess platform health without delay during incident response.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive operational documentation and context-sensitive help within the administrative interface, enabling a newly onboarded System Administrator or DBA to perform routine monitoring and error-triage tasks without external assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-sufficient operational documentation reduces dependency on vendor support and accelerates onboarding in a municipal IT environment with staff turnover.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all monitoring metrics, health status, and KPI data via a documented, versioned API (e.g., REST/JSON) to allow integration with the City's enterprise monitoring and alerting infrastructure.
generated_requirements.rationale: A documented metrics API enables the platform to be observed through existing City monitoring tooling, reducing operational silos.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all internal processing events — including message receipt, routing decisions, transformation outcomes, delivery attempts, retries, DLQ transitions, and acknowledgments — in a structured, machine-parseable format (e.g., JSON) to a centralized log store.
generated_requirements.rationale: Structured centralized logging is essential for observability, root-cause analysis, and integration with SIEM or log-aggregation tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configuration changes — including routing rules, transformation maps, retry policies, and queue parameters — through a version-controlled configuration management interface without requiring redeployment of the platform software.
generated_requirements.rationale: Runtime-configurable parameters reduce change risk and align with DoIT change-management requirements by avoiding full redeployments for operational tuning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on commodity x86-64 server hardware or a City-approved virtualization or container platform (e.g., VMware, Kubernetes), without dependency on proprietary hardware or operating system features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hardware and platform independence protects the City from vendor lock-in and aligns with DoIT procurement and infrastructure standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with external systems using industry-standard protocols (XML/SOAP and REST/JSON), and shall not require modifications to the CSR or EAM systems' existing integration interfaces to connect to the platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard protocol support and non-invasive integration preserve the contractual and operational integrity of existing City enterprise systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete planned maintenance operations — including software upgrades, configuration deployments, and node replacements — using rolling or blue-green deployment strategies that result in zero downtime for message processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Zero-downtime maintenance is required to meet the near-continuous availability SLA during regular business operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall replicate message data and configuration state across a minimum of two geographically or logically separated nodes to ensure no single point of failure exists in the message persistence layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-node replication eliminates single points of failure and ensures message durability in the event of hardware or network failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a disaster recovery capability with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 5 minutes and a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of no more than 30 minutes for a full platform failure scenario.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RPO and RTO targets ensure that platform recovery from catastrophic failure meets the operational continuity requirements of municipal service delivery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall mask or tokenize PII fields (e.g., citizen name, address, contact information) in all dashboard displays, log views, and exported reports accessible to roles other than System Administrator and DBA, unless explicit data-access justification is recorded.
generated_requirements.rationale: PII masking in operational views limits unnecessary exposure of citizen data and supports PIIPA compliance and breach-risk reduction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a self-service interface for authorized DBAs to execute ad-hoc queries against message metadata and audit logs using a structured query language or guided query builder, without requiring direct database access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ad-hoc query capability for DBAs supports operational troubleshooting and FOIA data extraction without exposing raw database credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a session timeout of no more than 30 minutes of inactivity for all administrative web dashboard sessions, requiring re-authentication upon timeout.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout controls are required by NIST SP 800-53 AC-11 to reduce the risk of unauthorized access via unattended sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an automated health-check endpoint that returns the current operational status of all queues, processing nodes, and integration connectors, and shall integrate with the City's enterprise monitoring platform via SNMP or REST-based health probes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated health-check endpoints enable proactive detection of component failures and integration with City-wide monitoring infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum message payload size limit (configurable by administrators, defaulting to 10 MB), rejecting oversized messages with a structured error response and logging the rejection event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Payload size limits protect platform stability and prevent resource exhaustion attacks or accidental oversized submissions from degrading throughput.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a documented, tested runbook for all common operational procedures — including node addition, DLQ remediation, configuration rollback, and FOIA data extraction — and shall store runbooks in a version-controlled repository accessible to authorized administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Version-controlled operational runbooks reduce mean time to resolution and ensure consistent procedure execution in a municipal IT environment with staff turnover.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall undergo a security vulnerability assessment and penetration test at least annually, and all identified critical or high-severity findings shall be remediated within 30 days of identification, with remediation evidence recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular security assessments and timely remediation are required by NIST SP 800-53 CA-8 and City IT security policy to maintain a defensible security posture for a platform handling municipal operational data.

domain: government enterprise integration / municipal IT infrastructure
sme_subject: enterprise middleware and systems integration architect with municipal government experience
sme_advisory: This platform sits at the heart of Chicago's municipal service delivery chain, brokering messages between citizen-facing CSR workflows and back-office asset management — meaning failures or data integrity lapses have direct public-service consequences and potential legal exposure. The requirements engineer should pay close attention to the interplay between strict SLA thresholds, government records-retention mandates, and the operational realities of a heterogeneous legacy IT estate typical of large municipal governments. Special emphasis should be placed on fault-tolerance patterns (dead-letter queues, idempotent retry, guaranteed delivery), role-based access controls aligned to city IT security policy, and the auditability obligations that arise from FOIA and municipal records law.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users exclusively via IBM's w3ID Single Sign-On (SSO) intranet authentication mechanism; no standalone or local credential store shall be used for user authentication.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM intranet SSO/w3ID integration is mandatory per IBM governance standards, and no independent credential store is permitted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles: read-only CCR Staff, CCR Management, and System Administrator, applying least-privilege principles so each role can only access the functions and data explicitly granted to it.
generated_requirements.rationale: Differentiating access levels between operational staff, management, and administrators ensures data security and appropriate information exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to the WCS application exclusively to users authenticated on the IBM intranet; no public internet access to the application or its data shall be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Limiting access to the IBM intranet boundary protects sensitive community grants data from unauthorized external exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized CCR users to generate summarized reports of community grants data by selecting report parameters through a point-and-click interface without requiring manual query construction.
generated_requirements.rationale: A point-and-click report generation interface is the core functional capability described for CCR users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support report filtering by at least the following parameters: geographic region, grant type, IBM fiscal year (February 1 – January 31), business unit, and grant recipient organization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-dimensional filtering enables CCR users and management to slice grants data according to their specific analytical needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall align all date ranges, period-over-period comparisons, and report labels to IBM's fiscal year calendar (February 1 – January 31) rather than the standard calendar year.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM's fiscal year differs from the calendar year, and all reporting must reflect this to ensure accurate financial and philanthropic analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide aggregated summary dashboards for CCR Management users, displaying high-level grant totals, trends, and key performance indicators across selectable dimensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Management requires summarized, high-level views to support strategic decision-making without navigating raw data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide detail-level tabular report views for CCR Staff users, enabling drill-down from summary figures to individual grant records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational CCR staff need access to granular grant-level data for day-to-day analysis and verification tasks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall refresh its underlying community grants dataset on a scheduled weekly basis, completing the refresh within a defined maintenance window that minimizes disruption to users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Weekly data refresh ensures the reporting tool reflects current grants information as described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clearly visible, human-readable timestamp on every report and dashboard indicating the date and time the underlying dataset was last successfully refreshed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be able to assess data currency at a glance to make informed decisions based on report outputs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support on-demand data refresh initiation by System Administrator users, in addition to the scheduled weekly refresh, with the refresh status and completion time logged and displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: On-demand refresh capability allows administrators to correct data currency issues outside the regular weekly schedule.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to export any generated report in Microsoft Excel format (.xlsx), preserving all column headers, numeric formatting, currency symbols, date formats, and special characters exactly as displayed in the on-screen report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Excel export fidelity is a core user requirement and ensures exported data is immediately usable without manual correction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to export any generated report in Lotus 1-2-3 format (.wk*), preserving all column headers, numeric formatting, currency symbols, date formats, and special characters exactly as displayed in the on-screen report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lotus 1-2-3 export is explicitly required by the use case to support users who rely on that spreadsheet application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify all exported reports containing sensitive financial or PII data according to IBM's internal data classification policy and apply appropriate handling labels to exported files before download.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM's data classification and export control policies require that sensitive financial data be labeled to govern downstream handling.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with IBM's internal data privacy policies and applicable regional privacy regulations, including GDPR for European grant data and CCPA for California-based grant recipients, when storing, displaying, and exporting any personally identifiable information (PII).
generated_requirements.rationale: Grant data may include PII of nonprofit contacts and recipients, requiring compliance with regional privacy laws to avoid legal and reputational risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall mask or restrict display of PII fields (e.g., individual recipient names, contact details) for CCR Staff users who do not have an explicit data access entitlement for PII, while still allowing aggregate reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Least-privilege enforcement requires that PII exposure be limited to only those roles with a documented need to access it.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log recording every user access event, report generation action, and data export event, capturing at minimum: user ID, timestamp, report parameters selected, and export format chosen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging is required to support security investigations, compliance audits, and accountability for access to sensitive grants data.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to IBM and CCR visual identity guidelines, including the use of approved color palettes, typography, logo placement, and layout standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to IBM and CCR branding standards ensures the tool is recognized as an official internal system and maintains corporate identity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with IBM's internal accessibility standards, which align with WCAG 2.1 Level AA, across all user-facing interfaces, including reports, dashboards, navigation, and export controls.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM mandates WCAG 2.1 AA compliance to ensure the tool is usable by employees with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: All UI color combinations used in the system shall meet or exceed the WCAG 2.1 AA minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text and UI components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessible contrast ratios are a specific WCAG 2.1 AA requirement and are explicitly called out in IBM's accessibility standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed such that a new authorized user can independently navigate the application, generate a report, and export data after a single use session, without requiring formal training or a user manual.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states the system must be intuitive enough for independent use after a single interaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text or tooltips for all report parameter fields, filter options, and export controls to guide users without requiring navigation away from the current page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance supports the single-use learnability goal by reducing user confusion at the point of interaction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display all monetary grant values with the correct currency symbol and ISO 4217 currency code for the currency in which the grant was disbursed, supporting multi-currency display across all reports and exports.
generated_requirements.rationale: As a worldwide system, grants are disbursed in multiple currencies and must be accurately represented to avoid financial misinterpretation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display data lineage and provenance information for each report, including the source system name, data extraction date, and a description of any transformation rules applied to the grant records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data lineage indicators allow users to assess the trustworthiness and origin of the data presented in reports.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the integrity of ingested grant data during each refresh cycle, rejecting or flagging records that fail defined data quality rules (e.g., missing mandatory fields, invalid currency codes, out-of-range dates), and notifying the System Administrator of any anomalies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity at the point of ingestion is critical for a read-oriented reporting tool where downstream decisions depend on accurate data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be hosted exclusively within IBM's approved internal hosting environment (IBM Cloud or approved IBM on-premises data centers) and shall not use unapproved third-party SaaS platforms for data storage or processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM's infrastructure governance policy prohibits use of unapproved external platforms for internal sensitive data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain grant records and associated audit logs in accordance with IBM's corporate records retention schedule, supporting multi-year retention of philanthropic disbursement data for audit and tax compliance purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: IBM's records retention policy mandates multi-year archival of philanthropic data for legal, audit, and tax purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide System Administrators with an interface to manage user role assignments, including adding, modifying, and revoking user role memberships, with all changes recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrators must be able to manage the RBAC model dynamically as staff roles and access needs change over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and display reports within 10 seconds of a user submitting report parameters for datasets up to the maximum expected data volume (defined during detailed design), under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Acceptable report generation response time is essential for user productivity and satisfaction in a reporting tool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the export of a report to Microsoft Excel or Lotus 1-2-3 format and make the file available for download within 30 seconds for reports containing up to 100,000 rows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Export operations must complete in a reasonable time to avoid user frustration and session timeouts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 50 concurrent authenticated users generating and viewing reports simultaneously without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: As an internal tool used by CCR employees globally, the system must handle concurrent usage without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% during IBM business hours (Monday–Friday, 06:00–22:00 local time across all supported IBM regions), excluding pre-announced maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: As a worldwide internal tool, high availability during business hours across global regions is required to support CCR operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify System Administrators via automated alert within 15 minutes of any unplanned service outage or failed data refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prompt notification of outages and refresh failures enables administrators to take corrective action before users are significantly impacted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit between the user's browser and the application server using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive grants and PII data from interception even within the IBM intranet.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all community grants data at rest using IBM-approved encryption standards, including PII fields and financial data stored in the underlying database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects sensitive data in the event of unauthorized physical or logical access to storage systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated user session after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication via IBM w3ID SSO before the session is resumed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access via unattended authenticated sessions on shared or unattended workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase, configuration, and deployment scripts shall be structured to allow a developer with IBM internal system knowledge to deploy a new instance or apply a patch within one business day.
generated_requirements.rationale: Maintainability ensures the system can be updated, patched, and redeployed efficiently as IBM's data and policy environment evolves.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on IBM's approved internal hosting infrastructure without modification to the core application code, supporting both IBM Cloud and approved on-premises environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability across IBM's approved hosting environments ensures the system can be migrated or redeployed as infrastructure decisions change.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based interface accessible via IBM-standard supported web browsers (at minimum: the current and previous major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari) without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser compatibility ensures all authorized IBM employees can access the system regardless of their standard-issue browser.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support keyboard-only navigation for all interactive elements, including report parameter selection, filter application, and export initiation, in compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA Success Criterion 2.1.1.
generated_requirements.rationale: Keyboard navigability is a core WCAG 2.1 AA requirement and ensures the tool is accessible to users who cannot use a mouse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure all non-text content (images, icons, charts) used in reports and dashboards includes programmatic alternative text descriptions compatible with screen reader assistive technologies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Alternative text for non-text content is required by WCAG 2.1 AA and IBM's accessibility standards to support visually impaired users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow CCR Management users to save and re-execute named report configurations (saved report templates) consisting of a specific combination of filter parameters, enabling repeated generation of standard reports without re-entering parameters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Saved report templates reduce repetitive effort for management users who regularly generate the same standard reports.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message and suggested corrective action whenever a report generation or export operation fails, without exposing internal system error details or stack traces to end users.
generated_requirements.rationale: User-friendly error handling supports the learnability goal and prevents exposure of sensitive system internals to end users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all data ingestion events during each refresh cycle, recording the source system, number of records ingested, number of records rejected, rejection reasons, and the timestamp of the completed refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ingestion event logging supports data lineage tracking, troubleshooting of data quality issues, and audit requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide System Administrators with a read-only audit log viewer interface that supports filtering of audit records by user ID, date range, event type, and report parameters.
generated_requirements.rationale: A searchable audit log viewer enables administrators to efficiently investigate access and export events for compliance and security purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support archival of grant records and audit logs that have exceeded their active retention period to IBM-approved long-term storage, with the ability to retrieve archived records upon authorized request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Archival and retrieval capability is required to meet IBM's corporate records retention schedule for multi-year philanthropic disbursement data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that report outputs and exported files accurately reflect the data as of the most recently completed weekly refresh, with no partial or inconsistent data states presented to users during an in-progress refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data consistency during refresh operations prevents users from making decisions based on incomplete or mixed-state datasets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from a failed scheduled data refresh without manual intervention, retrying the refresh up to three times before alerting the System Administrator of a persistent failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic retry logic for refresh failures minimizes data staleness and reduces the operational burden on administrators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a documented and tested data source integration interface (e.g., API or ETL specification) that defines how community grants data is extracted from upstream source systems, enabling future source system changes to be accommodated with minimal rework.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined integration interface reduces the cost and risk of adapting the system when upstream grants management systems change.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present all user-facing text, labels, and messages in English as the primary language, with the architecture designed to support future localization into additional languages without requiring core application code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: As a worldwide system, designing for future localization ensures the tool can be extended to non-English-speaking IBM regions without architectural rework.

sme_advisory: The Worldwide Community System (WCS) is an internal IBM reporting and analytics tool for Corporate Community Relations (CCR) grant data. Requirements engineers should pay close attention to IBM's internal governance standards, intranet security posture, and the nuanced reporting needs of a global philanthropy program — including multi-currency, multi-region, and fiscal-year alignment concerns. Because this is a read-oriented reporting tool (not a transactional grants management system), requirements should emphasize data integrity at the point of ingestion, export fidelity, and role-based access rather than workflow or approval processes.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall use IBM and CCR branding standards. The interface must have one IBM logo and one CCR logo.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be displayable on any work computer a CCR user may have. The system interface shall be formatted to display at 800 by 600 pixel resolution.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system interface will use CCR nomenclature. The system must use the same nomenclature used in the existing WCS system and reports
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The interface must summarize the data in a fashion that's appealing to CCR management. Every report type must have at least one high level summary shown in the results dashboard/interface
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system must use a point and click interface. 100% of all selection and navigation functionality must be able to be activated/selected by either a mouse or a keyboard
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The interface shall be easy to use. 90% of users should be able to successfully use the system to generate a report on their first use. The interface should adhere to IBM usability standards.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Reports must be easy to generate. Reports must be generated within one button click and exported within one button click (after selecting the report)
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A user shall be able to use the system without instruction after a short amount of time. After one use a user should understand and remember 90% of the system usage and navigation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All CCR users shall receive training before the WCS system is deployed. One training session will be held for 100% of CCR users before system deployment (based on a CCR user being available)
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall use appropriate nomenclature and terminology as defined by the Corporate Community Grants organization. All interfaces and reports will undergo usability tests by CCR users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall be available at all times as long as IBM's internal intranet W3 is accessible. The WCS system will be available 95% of the time IBM's internal intranet W3 is available.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall have the ability to connect other designated WCS databases currently not included in the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall have the ability to include data for regions outside the United States and be accessed by users outside of the United States if approved by CCR management.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall be run in a Windows environment. The WCS shall be accessed by users through the Internet on an IBM computer running a Windows 2000 or above operating system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Brio portion of the WCS system must be able to export files in spreadsheet form (Microsoft Excel and Lotus 1-2-3 formats). Brio will provide buttons in the user interface that produce/export reports in .xls or .123 file formats.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system must run queries to the WCS databases once again (on a specified day) to update the available data available to CCR users. Queries must be made to external databases for data refresh once a week.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All changes updates and additions to the WCS system shall be updated in the WCS Developer and WCS User Guides. Every change must be reflected in WCS system documentation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall only be accessed by authorized Corporate Community Grants (CCR) employees. Users must be authenticated using their IBM intranet user name and password.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The WCS system shall not allow automatic logins by any user. Cookies containing WCS login information about a user will not be stored on a user's computer.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with at minimum two distinct roles — Commissioner and Team Owner — where each role is granted only the permissions explicitly assigned to it and denied all others by default.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clear permission boundaries between commissioner and team-owner roles are essential to prevent unauthorized governance actions and protect league integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to configure league-wide settings including league name, number of teams, playoff structure, trade deadline (aligned to a specific NFL calendar week), waiver wire type (standard priority or FAAB), and scoring format (PPR, half-PPR, standard, or custom) before and during the season.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioners require full governance control over league parameters to properly administer the league throughout the NFL season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall version-snapshot all fantasy point scoring rule configurations at the time of each change, preserving the full history of rule sets with timestamps and the identity of the Commissioner who made each change, so that historical scores can be audited against the rules in effect at the time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned scoring rule snapshots enable accurate dispute resolution and ensure retroactive score recalculations use the correct rule set.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to define and modify custom scoring rules including but not limited to: passing yards per point threshold, receiving yards per point threshold, PPR value (0, 0.5, or 1.0), touchdown point values, and bonus thresholds (e.g., 100-yard bonus), with changes taking effect from a commissioner-specified NFL week.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible scoring configurability is a core differentiator for league management platforms and accommodates diverse league preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Team Owners to add available free-agent NFL players to their roster, subject to roster size limits, positional constraints, and waiver wire rules configured by the Commissioner.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player adds are a fundamental roster-management action that team owners must be able to perform within league-defined constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Team Owners to drop players from their roster, making those players immediately available as free agents or placing them on the waiver wire according to the league's configured waiver type and processing schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player drops are a core roster action and must correctly transition player availability state per league waiver rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process all roster transactions (adds, drops, trades) atomically, using serialized locking per player per league, such that no NFL player can be simultaneously owned by more than one team in the same league at any point in time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic, conflict-free transaction processing prevents duplicate player ownership, which would corrupt league standings and fairness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit log of all roster transactions (adds, drops, trades), scoring overrides, and Commissioner setting changes, recording the timestamp (UTC), actor identity (user ID and display name), action type, and before/after state for each event.
generated_requirements.rationale: An auditable transaction history is required for dispute resolution and provides accountability for all privileged and standard actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a waiver wire rule engine configurable by the Commissioner, including: standard waiver priority order (with automatic or manual reordering after claims), FAAB blind-bidding mode with a commissioner-defined budget cap per team, waiver processing day and time (defaulting to Wednesday 03:00 AM local time), and waiver claim conflict resolution (highest priority or highest bid wins).
generated_requirements.rationale: A configurable waiver rule engine is central to fair free-agent acquisition and must support the most common league formats.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to manually adjust any team's waiver priority position or FAAB balance at any time, with the adjustment recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioners occasionally need to correct waiver order or budget errors, and all such changes must be traceable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Team Owners to propose trades to one or more other Team Owners, specifying the players and/or FAAB budget being exchanged, with the receiving team owner able to accept, counter-offer, or reject the trade proposal.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trade proposals are a core engagement and roster-management feature requiring a full offer/counter/reject lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable trade review window (1–3 days, set by the Commissioner) during which accepted trades are held pending veto review before roster changes are executed, and shall enforce a hard trade deadline aligned to a Commissioner-specified NFL calendar week after which no new trades may be proposed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trade review windows and deadlines protect league integrity and align with standard fantasy football governance practices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two configurable trade veto mechanisms: (a) Commissioner veto, where the Commissioner may unilaterally reject a trade during the review window with a mandatory written reason, and (b) League-vote veto, where a configurable majority of non-involved team owners (e.g., >50%) must vote to veto within the review window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple veto mechanisms accommodate different league governance preferences and prevent collusive or unbalanced trades.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to manually override a player's fantasy point score for a given scoring period, with the override recorded in the audit log including the original score, overridden score, reason text, and Commissioner identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Score overrides are needed to correct data feed errors and must be fully auditable to maintain trust in league outcomes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ingest player statistics from a licensed NFL data feed provider (e.g., Sportradar or Stats Perform) and display play-by-play fantasy point updates with a maximum end-to-end latency of 60 seconds from the time of the NFL game event to the time the updated score is visible to all users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-real-time score updates are a primary user expectation during live games and directly affect roster decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display each NFL player's current-season statistics (e.g., passing yards, rushing yards, receptions, touchdowns) and cumulative fantasy points on the player detail page, refreshed at least every 60 seconds during active NFL game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Up-to-date player statistics are essential for informed roster decisions and are a core feature of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce all contractual obligations of the licensed NFL data feed agreement, including: prohibiting redistribution of raw feed data to third parties, displaying required data-provider attribution on all statistics pages, and purging or expiring player statistical data in accordance with the data retention terms specified in the licensing agreement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Violation of NFL data feed licensing terms exposes the platform to contract termination and legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that all NFL team names, logos, and player likenesses displayed in the UI are covered by a valid sublicense from NFL Properties LLC, and shall not render any unlicensed NFL intellectual property.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unlicensed use of NFL trademarks and player likenesses constitutes trademark infringement and exposes the platform to legal action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a league message board where any league participant (Commissioner or Team Owner) can create posts and reply to existing posts, with posts displayed in reverse-chronological order and the five most recent posts surfaced on the main league page.
generated_requirements.rationale: A message board drives community engagement and keeps participants informed of league activity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Commissioner with message board moderation controls including: pinning a post to the top of the board, deleting any post or reply, and locking a thread to prevent further replies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Moderation controls are necessary to maintain a respectful and on-topic community environment within the league.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow any Team Owner to report a message board post or reply as spam or abusive content, triggering a notification to the Commissioner for review and action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peer-reporting mechanisms supplement commissioner moderation and help surface harmful content quickly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send in-app and email notifications to relevant users for the following events: trade proposal received, trade accepted/rejected/vetoed, waiver claim result, roster transaction completed, new message board post in their league, and Commissioner setting changes that affect their team.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications keep users engaged and informed of actions that require their attention or affect their team.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide intuitive navigation allowing users to move between the league page, team roster page, player search/add page, standings page, matchup page, and message board in no more than two clicks or taps from any primary page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Efficient navigation reduces friction and supports the rapid decision-making that fantasy football users require during live game windows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards across all web pages, including sufficient color contrast ratios, keyboard navigability, screen-reader-compatible ARIA labels, and accessible form controls.
generated_requirements.rationale: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance is a legal baseline in multiple jurisdictions and ensures the platform is usable by people with disabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a page load time of 2 seconds or less (measured as Time to Interactive) for all primary pages (league page, team roster page, player list page) under normal load conditions, and no more than 4 seconds under peak NFL-season load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast page load times are critical to user satisfaction and retention, especially during high-traffic game-day periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain 99.9% uptime availability (measured monthly), with planned maintenance windows scheduled exclusively between Tuesday 02:00–06:00 AM ET (the lowest-traffic period of the NFL week), and shall not schedule maintenance during Thursday through Monday during the NFL regular season and playoffs.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability aligned to the NFL calendar ensures the platform is accessible during the peak engagement periods that define the product's value.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be capable of handling at least 10,000 concurrent authenticated user sessions without degradation in response time beyond the thresholds defined in R025, and shall auto-scale horizontally to accommodate traffic spikes of up to 5× baseline during Sunday afternoon NFL game windows and waiver wire processing periods.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seasonal, event-driven traffic spikes (Sunday games, waiver processing) require quantified scalability targets to ensure platform stability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher) for all client-server communications, and shall reject any connection attempts using deprecated protocols (SSL, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1).
generated_requirements.rationale: Encrypting all communications protects user credentials, personal data, and transaction integrity from interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require users to authenticate via a username/password credential pair with passwords meeting a minimum complexity policy (at least 8 characters, including uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character), and shall offer optional multi-factor authentication (MFA) via TOTP or SMS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication protects user accounts from unauthorized access and safeguards sensitive league and personal data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement an age-gate during user registration that requires users to confirm they are 13 years of age or older; if a user indicates they are under 13, registration shall be blocked and a COPPA-compliant parental consent flow shall be presented before any account is created.
generated_requirements.rationale: COPPA mandates verifiable parental consent for collection of personal information from children under 13.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a GDPR-compliant consent notice to users identified as EU residents at registration, explicitly requesting consent for data processing and marketing communications as separate, unbundled opt-in choices, and shall provide a mechanism for users to withdraw consent at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR requires freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous consent for personal data processing from EU-resident users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide all users with the ability to: (a) export a machine-readable copy of their personal data (GDPR/CCPA data portability), (b) request deletion of their account and associated personal data within 30 days (right to erasure), and (c) opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal data (CCPA).
generated_requirements.rationale: GDPR and CCPA impose legally enforceable data subject rights that the platform must operationalize for EU and California residents.

generated_requirements.text: If the platform collects entry fees or distributes prize payouts via credit or debit card, the system shall process all payment card transactions through a PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant payment processor, shall not store raw card numbers or CVV values, and shall display a PCI-DSS compliance attestation in the platform's legal/privacy documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS compliance is mandatory for any platform handling payment card data and protects users from financial fraud.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include a legal classification disclosure in its terms of service documenting that the platform operates as a game of skill under applicable U.S. Daily Fantasy Sports regulations, and shall implement state-level access restrictions (blocking registration or paid-league participation) for users in jurisdictions where season-long fantasy sports with entry fees are prohibited.
generated_requirements.rationale: DFS regulations in states such as New York, Texas, and Louisiana require explicit legal classification and geographic access controls for paid leagues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Team Owners to view their current roster, including each player's name, NFL team, position, injury status, bye week, current-week projected fantasy points, and season-to-date fantasy points, on a single roster management page.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive roster view consolidates the information team owners need to make informed lineup and transaction decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Team Owners to set their weekly starting lineup by assigning players to active roster slots and bench slots before the Commissioner-configured lineup lock time (defaulting to the kickoff time of the first game in which a rostered player participates each week).
generated_requirements.rationale: Lineup setting is the primary weekly action for team owners and must enforce lock times to prevent unfair post-kickoff changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a searchable and filterable player pool showing all NFL players not currently on a roster in the league, with filters for position, NFL team, availability status (free agent or on waivers), and sortable columns for projected points, season points, and recent performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: An effective player search interface is essential for team owners to identify and evaluate available players for roster additions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a weekly matchup page for each team showing the head-to-head opponent, live scoring updates for all active players on both rosters, and the current score differential, refreshed at the same interval as the player statistics feed (maximum 60-second latency).
generated_requirements.rationale: Live matchup scoring is the primary engagement surface during NFL game windows and must reflect real-time data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a league standings page showing all teams ranked by win-loss-tie record, with tiebreakers applied in the order configured by the Commissioner (e.g., total points scored, head-to-head record), updated after each scoring period is finalized.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standings are a core league feature that drives competition and must accurately reflect current results with correct tiebreaker logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a configurable playoff bracket generated automatically at the end of the regular season based on standings, with the Commissioner able to define the number of playoff teams (4, 6, or 8), seeding rules, and playoff week schedule aligned to the NFL calendar.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated playoff bracket generation reduces commissioner workload and ensures consistent application of league rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement application-layer rate limiting on all authenticated API endpoints, rejecting requests that exceed 100 calls per minute per user session with an HTTP 429 response, to protect against automated abuse and ensure fair resource allocation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rate limiting prevents automated scraping, credential stuffing, and denial-of-service attacks on the platform's APIs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all user passwords as salted hashes using a memory-hard hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt with cost factor ≥12, or Argon2id), and shall never store or log plaintext passwords.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage protects user credentials in the event of a database breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable across multiple geographic availability zones within a cloud provider's infrastructure, with automatic failover to a healthy zone within 30 seconds of a zone-level failure, ensuring no single point of failure for any critical service component.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-zone deployment with automatic failover is required to meet the 99.9% availability SLA during the NFL season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at minimum every 6 hours during the NFL season (September–February), with point-in-time recovery capability and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour and a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of no more than 2 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Frequent backups and defined RPO/RTO targets protect against data loss and ensure rapid recovery from infrastructure failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with documented internal APIs between components (data ingestion, scoring engine, roster transaction service, user management, notification service), enabling independent deployment and testing of each module.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the blast radius of changes and enables teams to update individual components without full-system redeployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any major cloud provider (AWS, Azure, or GCP) using containerized services (Docker/Kubernetes) without requiring provider-specific proprietary services as hard dependencies, ensuring cloud portability.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud portability prevents vendor lock-in and allows the platform to migrate providers if commercial or technical conditions change.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Commissioner dashboard that aggregates league health indicators including: pending trade reviews, unresolved abuse reports, upcoming waiver processing times, teams with incomplete lineups, and recent audit log entries, all visible on a single screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated commissioner dashboard reduces the administrative burden of league governance and surfaces time-sensitive actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully degrade during partial data feed outages by displaying the last successfully ingested player statistics with a clearly visible staleness indicator (e.g., 'Stats last updated 5 minutes ago') rather than showing blank or error states.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation maintains usability during upstream data feed disruptions and keeps users informed of data freshness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all security-relevant events — including failed login attempts, permission-denied actions, admin privilege escalations, and data export requests — to a tamper-evident, append-only security event log retained for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: A durable security event log supports incident investigation, regulatory compliance, and detection of unauthorized access patterns.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a mobile-responsive web interface that renders correctly and is fully functional on screen widths from 320px (small mobile) to 2560px (large desktop), without requiring a separate native mobile application.
generated_requirements.rationale: A responsive web interface ensures accessibility across the diverse range of devices used by fantasy football participants, particularly during mobile game-day usage.

sme_subject: fantasy sports league operations and NFL data licensing specialist
sme_advisory: Fantasy football league management platforms sit at the intersection of real-time sports data licensing, consumer gaming regulations, and high-concurrency web application design. The requirements engineer should pay close attention to NFL data feed SLAs and licensing restrictions, role-based access control separating commissioner and team-owner privileges, and the transactional integrity of roster moves (adds, drops, trades) that must be atomic and auditable. Given the seasonal, event-driven traffic spikes (e.g., waiver wire processing Sunday night, Thursday kickoff rushes), scalability and availability requirements must be quantified against specific NFL calendar milestones rather than stated generically.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Each team owner will need to use a username and password in order to be authenticated to reach the team owner's page
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Commissioner will be the only authorized user per league to adjust league settings.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Each page shall contain a navigation menu which shows the index into which the current page is contained.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Each page shall contain a similar format with action items in one location and navigation buttons in a different location.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The message board shall contain the 5 most recently posted messages on the league page. The messages will contain up to the first 150 characters of the message.

ground_truth_requirements.text: A new user shall be able to navigate through the league and team pages within 30 seconds of reaching the start-up page.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Users shall be able to reach the 'add' , drop',  and 'trade player' pages with one click from the team owner's page.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Users shall be able to view at least 25 NFL player statistics and points on each page.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall ask the user to confirm dropping a player or trading a player before performing the action.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall be able to complete every user transaction within one fourth of a second of receiving the request over the network.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall have a downtime of at most 10 minutes per year.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall run on a web application server.

ground_truth_requirements.text: A primary database will be used to store cached data for active users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A secondary database will be used to store historical statistics and data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Application updates shall occur between 3AM and 6 AM CST on Wednesday morning during the middle of the NFL season.

ground_truth_requirements.text: New System version releases shall be released at the beginning of each NFL season.
