generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate and authorize all users exclusively through integration with the central User Administration System (UAS); the RFS must not maintain its own independent user credential store.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralizing identity management in UAS ensures consistent access provisioning and deprovisioning across the enterprise and eliminates credential duplication risks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) tied to the PCG organizational hierarchy levels (branch, complex, district, regional), ensuring each user can only view, create, edit, or approve budget data within the scope of their authorized organizational unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Budget data visibility and edit rights differ materially across hierarchy levels; enforcing scope-limited RBAC prevents unauthorized cross-unit data access or manipulation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the following defined budget submission workflow states: Draft, Submitted, Under Review, Approved, and Rejected. State transitions shall be enforced according to role-based rules, preventing unauthorized state changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A governed workflow with explicit states and role-gated transitions ensures budget submissions follow the correct approval chain across all hierarchy levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically aggregate and roll up approved lower-level budget submissions (branch → complex → district → regional) using configurable aggregation rules, updating parent-level budget views in real time or near-real time upon child-level approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical roll-up is essential for regional and district managers to obtain consolidated budget views without manual re-entry of subordinate data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load revenue and expense data from the Business Data Warehouse (BDW) as the authoritative source of record. Any manual override of BDW-sourced data must be explicitly flagged, require a mandatory justification entry, and be stored separately in an auditable override log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Treating BDW as the authoritative source and flagging overrides preserves data integrity and ensures deviations from source data are transparent and auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the timestamp of the most recent BDW data load on all relevant budget entry screens, enabling users to assess the currency of the data they are working with.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users need to know how current their source data is to make informed budgeting decisions and identify whether a refresh is needed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized finance administrators to manually trigger an immediate BDW data refresh or schedule recurring automated refreshes, with configurable frequency aligned to the active budgeting calendar.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance admins must be able to control data freshness, especially during the critical budgeting window when timely BDW data is operationally significant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform a reconciliation check after each BDW data load, comparing loaded record counts and control totals against expected values, and shall alert finance administrators of any discrepancies before making the loaded data available to users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reconciliation after each load prevents corrupted or incomplete BDW data from propagating into budget submissions, protecting data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support annual formula versioning, allowing authorized administrators to create, update, and publish new formula versions prior to each budgeting cycle without disrupting budget submissions that are already in progress under a prior formula version.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula updates must be isolated from in-progress submissions to prevent mid-cycle recalculation errors that could invalidate partially completed budgets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain a complete version history of all budget calculation formulae, including the formula definition, the administrator who published it, and the effective date range for each version.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula version history supports auditability and allows reconstruction of how any historical budget submission was calculated.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a comprehensive audit trail for all budget data entries, edits, manual overrides, formula updates, workflow state transitions, and approval actions. Each audit record shall capture: user identity, timestamp, action type, affected record identifier, previous value, and new value.
generated_requirements.rationale: A complete audit trail is required for financial governance, regulatory compliance, and the ability to investigate discrepancies or disputes in budget submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all budget submissions, audit logs, manual override records, and formula version histories for a minimum of seven (7) years, in compliance with PCG's internal records retention policy and applicable financial regulatory requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seven-year retention is a standard accounting regulatory requirement and ensures PCG can satisfy audit and legal discovery obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable submission deadline cut-off dates for each organizational level. After the cut-off, the system shall prevent new submissions and warn users attempting to submit. Authorized administrators shall have the ability to override the cut-off for specific organizational units with a mandatory justification recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deadline enforcement ensures the budgeting calendar is adhered to, while a governed override mechanism accommodates legitimate exceptions without bypassing accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically flag budget line items where the submitted value deviates from the prior-year actual or system-calculated benchmark by more than a configurable threshold percentage, and shall present these exceptions prominently in a variance and exception report accessible to reviewers and approvers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated variance flagging reduces manual review effort and ensures material deviations receive appropriate scrutiny before approval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized administrators to configure variance threshold percentages independently for revenue line items and expense line items, and to set different thresholds per organizational hierarchy level.
generated_requirements.rationale: Different line item types and organizational levels may have materially different acceptable variance ranges, requiring granular threshold configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support configuration of the annual budgeting calendar, including formula update windows, BDW data load schedules, submission open and close dates, and approval deadlines, aligned to PCG's fiscal year boundaries which may differ from the calendar year.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCG's fiscal year may not align with the calendar year, so all time-sensitive system processes must be configurable rather than hardcoded to calendar dates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display budget data with numeric precision conforming to PCG's chart of accounts standards, including currency rounding rules and the required decimal precision for revenue versus expense line items.
generated_requirements.rationale: Incorrect rounding or precision can introduce material financial errors in aggregated budget figures, violating PCG's accounting standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to ENET Securities' published intranet UI/UX standards for all screens, including layout conventions, color schemes, typography, navigation patterns, and component styles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conformance to ENET Securities' intranet standards ensures visual and interaction consistency with other internal applications, reducing user learning curve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be compatible with all web browsers specified in ENET Securities' current intranet browser compatibility standards, including the minimum supported versions of each listed browser.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser compatibility with ENET Securities' approved browser list ensures all finance staff can access the system regardless of their workstation configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to the accessibility guidelines specified in ENET Securities' intranet standards, ensuring that all screens and interactive components are usable by staff with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility conformance is required by ENET Securities' intranet standards and ensures equitable access for all finance staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of no less than 99.5% during the defined annual budgeting window (from the opening of the submission period to the final approval deadline), as measured on a monthly basis excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: The budgeting window is a time-critical period; system unavailability during this period can have material financial and operational consequences for PCG.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of no less than 99.0% outside of the defined annual budgeting window, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: A baseline availability SLA outside the peak budgeting period ensures the system remains accessible for administrative tasks and reporting year-round.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated page load or data retrieval request within three (3) seconds at the 95th percentile under the expected peak concurrent user load during the budgeting window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance during peak load is critical to prevent bottlenecks that could delay budget submissions across the organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 200 concurrent active users during the peak budgeting period without degradation of response times below the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance staff across all hierarchy levels submit budgets simultaneously during the budgeting window, requiring the system to handle significant concurrent load.

generated_requirements.text: All data exchanged between the RFS and the BDW, and between the RFS and the UAS, shall use approved enterprise integration protocols as defined by PCG's integration architecture standards, and must not expose sensitive financial or user data outside the intranet boundary.
generated_requirements.rationale: Using approved protocols and restricting data to the intranet boundary prevents unauthorized external exposure of sensitive financial and identity data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all sensitive financial data at rest using an encryption standard approved by PCG's information security policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects sensitive budget and financial data from unauthorized access in the event of storage media compromise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit between the client browser and the server, and between the RFS and integrated systems (BDW, UAS), using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encrypting data in transit prevents interception of sensitive financial and authentication data on the intranet network.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated user session after a configurable period of inactivity, requiring re-authentication via UAS before the session is resumed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access to budget data from unattended workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate UAS access provisioning and deprovisioning changes (e.g., role assignments, role removals, account deactivations) to the RFS within a configurable synchronization interval not to exceed one (1) hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely synchronization of UAS changes ensures that users who have left a role or the organization cannot retain access to budget data beyond the defined window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a budget entry interface that allows branch-level users to enter, edit, and save revenue and expense line items pre-populated with BDW-sourced actuals, with inline calculation of formula-driven fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-population from BDW and inline formula calculation reduces manual data entry effort and minimizes input errors at the branch level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide complex-level users with a consolidated view of all subordinate branch budget submissions within their complex, including aggregated totals and the submission status of each branch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Complex managers need visibility into the status and totals of all branch submissions to manage their review and approval responsibilities effectively.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide district-level users with a consolidated view of all subordinate complex budget submissions within their district, including aggregated totals and submission statuses.
generated_requirements.rationale: District managers require a rolled-up view across complexes to perform their review and approval functions within the budget workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide regional-level users with a consolidated view of all subordinate district budget submissions within their region, including aggregated totals, submission statuses, and variance exception summaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regional managers need the highest-level consolidated view to assess overall budget health and make final approval decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify users via an in-application alert and configurable email notification when a budget submission assigned to them changes state (e.g., submitted for their review, approved, or rejected), including the reason for rejection when applicable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications ensure that workflow participants are aware of actions required of them, preventing delays in the budget approval chain.

generated_requirements.text: When a budget submission is rejected, the system shall require the rejecting user to enter a mandatory rejection reason, which shall be recorded in the audit log and communicated to the submitting user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mandatory rejection reasons provide submitters with actionable feedback and create an auditable record of why a submission was not approved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a rejected budget submission to be revised and resubmitted by the originating user, with the revision history and all prior rejection reasons retained in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting resubmission after rejection is a core workflow requirement; retaining history ensures full traceability of the submission lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide authorized finance administrators with a dashboard displaying the real-time status of all budget submissions across the organizational hierarchy, including counts by workflow state and identification of overdue submissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: An administrative dashboard enables finance staff to monitor budgeting cycle progress and proactively address bottlenecks or missed deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate exportable variance and exception reports in at least two formats (e.g., PDF and CSV/Excel), filterable by organizational unit, hierarchy level, line item type, and variance threshold breach.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable reports in standard formats allow finance staff to share and further analyze exception data outside the system using common office tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a user from submitting a budget that contains unresolved data validation errors (e.g., missing mandatory fields, values outside permissible ranges, formula calculation errors), and shall display clear, field-level error messages identifying each issue.
generated_requirements.rationale: Blocking submission of invalid data and providing clear error messages protects data integrity and guides users to correct issues before submission.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized administrators to configure the organizational hierarchy structure (branches, complexes, districts, regions) and the mapping of users to organizational units, with all changes logged in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: The organizational hierarchy may change between budgeting cycles; administrators must be able to update the structure without requiring system code changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts and unauthorized access attempts, and shall alert the security administration team when a configurable threshold of failed attempts is exceeded within a defined time window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Monitoring and alerting on failed access attempts enables early detection of brute-force or unauthorized access activity targeting the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that annual formula updates, budgeting calendar reconfigurations, and organizational hierarchy changes can be performed by authorized administrators through the application's configuration interface without requiring code deployments or vendor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Annual reconfiguration tasks must be self-serviceable by finance administrators to avoid operational dependency on development teams during time-critical budgeting cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete a full BDW data load and reconciliation process within a maximum of two (2) hours, ensuring data is available to users within an acceptable window relative to the scheduled load time.
generated_requirements.rationale: A bounded data load duration ensures that BDW refreshes do not create extended periods of stale data during the active budgeting window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a read-only historical budget view, allowing authorized users to access and compare finalized budget submissions from prior fiscal years within their authorized organizational scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access to prior-year budgets supports year-over-year comparison and informed decision-making during the current budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement input sanitization and protection against common web application vulnerabilities (including SQL injection, cross-site scripting, and cross-site request forgery) on all user-facing input fields and API endpoints.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting against common web vulnerabilities is essential for an intranet financial application handling sensitive budget data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduled maintenance windows that can be configured outside of the active budgeting period, and shall display a configurable advance notice message to users prior to any planned downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-announced maintenance windows minimize disruption to users and ensure maintenance activities are not inadvertently scheduled during the critical budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture such that the BDW integration layer, UAS integration layer, formula engine, and workflow engine are independently deployable and testable components.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the risk that changes to one integration or component will introduce regressions in unrelated system areas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text and tooltips on all budget entry fields, formula-driven fields, and workflow action buttons, explaining the expected input, calculation basis, or action consequence.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline help reduces user errors and support requests by providing guidance at the point of need, particularly for less experienced finance staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full audit log search and export interface accessible to authorized finance administrators, supporting filtering by user, date range, action type, organizational unit, and record identifier, with export to CSV.
generated_requirements.rationale: A searchable and exportable audit log is essential for internal audit reviews, regulatory examinations, and investigation of data discrepancies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backup of all budget submission data, audit logs, formula versions, and configuration data, with backups retained for the duration of the records retention period and tested for restorability on a quarterly basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular, tested backups ensure that financial data can be recovered in the event of system failure, data corruption, or accidental deletion.

domain: financial budgeting and enterprise resource planning (ERP)
sme_subject: corporate finance and budgeting process analyst
sme_advisory: The Revenue and Expense Forecasting System (RFS) operates within a tightly governed corporate finance environment where data integrity, role-based access, and integration reliability are paramount. Requirements engineers should pay close attention to the hierarchical nature of PCG's organizational structure, as budget data aggregation, delegation, and visibility rules will differ significantly across branch, complex, district, and regional levels — a nuance that generalist engineers frequently underspecify. Special care must also be taken around the annual formula update cycle, BDW data load processes, and peak-period system availability, as failures in any of these areas during the budgeting window can have material financial and operational consequences.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be available 24/7 especially during the budgeting period. The RFS system shall be available 90% of the time all year and 98% during the budgeting period. 2% of the time the system will become available within 1 hour of the time that the situation is reported.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum hardware requirements prior to implementation. Minimum Hard Disk Space: 400MB.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum software requirements prior to implementation: Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.0. Microsoft .NET Framework 1.1. Microsoft Internet Explorer Web Controls
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be able to easily interface with the BDW environment in order to load the data. Seven data files shall be received from the BDW and loaded into the RFS system within 3 hours one day prior to the start of budgeting process every year. The data will be as of October month-end. Test files (7) shall be loaded up to 5 times throughout the year from the BDW.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Changes to revenue and expense formulae must be applied to the RFS system every year prior to the budgeting process start. Revenue and expense formulae will be updated within one week of their receipt after which 2 weeks will be allotted to testing of the budget calculations to be conducted jointly by the project development team and PCG Finance.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: New branches/complexes/districts/regions and revenue/expense accounts added to the BDW must be reflected in the RFS system. 100% of the entities (mentioned above) available as of October month-end will be loaded into the RFS system prior to the budget start process.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only authorized user shall access the RFS system. ONLY users that exist in the UAS system with the RFS system assigned to their role(s) shall access the RFS system and use it.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: User access should be limited to the permissions granted to their role(s) Each level in the PCG hierarchy will be assigned a role and users will be assigned to these roles. Access to functionality within RFS system is dependent on the privileges/permission assigned to the role.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must comply with the intranet page standards and requirements of ENET Securities. 95% of the product look & feel will be approved by the Architecture group prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected and approved within one month of product release.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The intranet pages should display appropriately in all resolutions from 800x600 and higher. The display of all the pages will be correct in 95% of the resolutions prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected within a week of notification of the incorrect display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The users should be able to easily use the system to successfully complete their budgets within the stipulated time for completion. With a week of training prior to product implementation 98% of the users shall complete their budgets within the stipulated time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product interface should be fast. The response time of the product interface should not exceed 2 seconds 90% of the time. For 10% of the time the response time should not be more than 10 seconds.not be more than 10 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR
